The following configurations control the flush of data to disk.\n    84→# There are a few important trade-offs here:\n    85→#    1. Durability: Unflushed data may be lost if you are not using replication.\n    86→#    2. Latency: Very large flush intervals may lead to latency spikes when the flush does occur as there will be a lot of data to flush.\n    87→#    3. Throughput: The flush is generally the most expensive operation, and a small flush interval may lead to excessive seeks.\n    88→# The settings below allow one to configure the flush policy to flush data after a period of time or\n    89→# every N messages (or both). This can be done globally and overridden on a per-topic basis.\n    90→\n    91→# The number of messages to accept before forcing a flush of data to disk\n    92→#log.flush.interval.messages=10000\n    93→\n    94→# The maximum amount of time a message can sit in a log before we force a flush\n    95→#log.flush.interval.ms=1000\n    96→\n    97→############################# Log Retention Policy #############################\n    98→\n    99→# The following configurations control the disposal of log segments. Java 8 support project-wide has been deprecated since Apache Kafka 3.0, Java 11 support for\n    10→the broker and tools has been deprecated since Apache Kafka 3.7 and removal of both is planned for Apache Kafka 4.0 (\n    11→see [KIP-750](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=181308223) and\n    12→[KIP-1013](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=284789510) for more details).\n    13→\n    14→Scala 2.12 and 2.13 are supported and 2.13 is used by default. Scala 2.12 support has been deprecated since\n    15→Apache Kafka 3.0 and will be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0 (see [KIP-751](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=181308218)\n    16→for more details). See below for how to use a specific Scala version or all of the supported Scala versions.\n    17→\n    18→### Build a jar and run it ###\n    19→    ./gradlew jar\n    20→\n    21→Follow instructions in https://kafka.apache.org/quickstart\n    22→\n    23→### Build source jar ###\n    24→    ./gradlew srcJar\n    25→\n    26→### Build aggregated javadoc ###\n    27→    ./gradlew aggregatedJavadoc\n    28→\n    29→### Build javadoc and scaladoc ###\n    30→    ./gradlew javadoc\n    31→    ./gradlew javadocJar # builds a javadoc jar for each module\n    32→    ./gradlew scaladoc\n    33→    ./gradlew scaladocJar # builds a scaladoc jar for each module\n    34→    ./gradlew docsJar # builds both (if applicable) javadoc and scaladoc jars for each module\n    35→\n    36→### Run unit/integration tests ###\n    37→    ./gradlew test # runs both unit and integration tests\n    38→    ./gradlew unitTest\n    39→    ./gradlew integrationTest\n    40→    \n    41→### Force re-running tests without code change ###\n    42→    ./gradlew test --rerun\n    43→    ./gradlew unitTest --rerun\n    44→    ./gradlew integrationTest --rerun\n    45→\n    46→### Running a particular unit/integration test ###\n    47→    ./gradlew clients:test --tests RequestResponseTest\n    48→\n    49→### Repeatedly running a particular unit/integration test ###\n    50→    I=0; while ./gradlew clients:test --tests RequestResponseTest --rerun --fail-fast; do (( I=$I+1 )); echo \"Completed run: $I\"; sleep 1; done\n    51→\n    52→### Running a particular test method within a unit/integration test ###\n    53→    ./gradlew core:test --tests kafka.api.ProducerFailureHandlingTest.testCannotSendToInternalTopic\n    54→    ./gradlew clients:test --tests org.apache.kafka.clients.MetadataTest.testTimeToNextUpdate\n    55→\n    56→### Running a particular unit/integration test with log4j output ###\n    57→By default, there will be only small number of logs output while testing. You can adjust it by changing the `log4j.properties` file in the module's `src/test/resources` directory.\n    58→\n    59→For example, if you want to see more logs for clients project tests, you can modify [the line](https://github.com/apache/kafka/blob/trunk/clients/src/test/resources/log4j.properties#L21) in `clients/src/test/resources/log4j.properties` \n    60→to `log4j.logger.org.apache.kafka=INFO` and then run:\n    61→    \n    62→    ./gradlew cleanTest clients:test --tests NetworkClientTest   \n    63→\n    64→And you should see `INFO` level logs in the file under the `clients/build/test-results/test` directory.\n    65→\n    66→### Specifying test retries ###\n    67→By default, each failed test is retried once up to a maximum of five retries per test run. Tests are retried at the end of the test task. Adjust these parameters in the following way:\n    68→\n    69→    ./gradlew test -PmaxTestRetries=1 -PmaxTestRetryFailures=5\n    70→    \n    71→See [Test Retry Gradle Plugin](https://github.com/gradle/test-retry-gradle-plugin) for more details.\n    72→\n    73→### Generating test coverage reports ###\n    74→Generate coverage reports for the whole project:\n    75→\n    76→    ./gradlew reportCoverage -PenableTestCoverage=true -Dorg.gradle.parallel=false\n    77→\n    78→Generate coverage for a single module, i.e.: \n    79→\n    80→    ./gradlew clients:reportCoverage -PenableTestCoverage=true -Dorg.gradle.parallel=false\n    81→    \n    82→### Building a binary release gzipped tar ball ###\n    83→    ./gradlew clean releaseTarGz\n    84→\n    85→The release file can be found inside `./core/build/distributions/`.\n    86→\n    87→### Building auto generated messages ###\n    88→Sometimes it is only necessary to rebuild the RPC auto-generated message data when switching between branches, as they could\n    89→fail due to code changes. You can just run:\n    90→ \n    91→    ./gradlew processMessages processTestMessages\n    92→\n    93→### Running a Kafka broker in KRaft mode\n    94→\n    95→Using compiled files:\n    96→\n    97→    KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID=\"$(./bin/kafka-storage.sh random-uuid)\"\n    98→    ./bin/kafka-storage.sh format -t $KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID -c config/kraft/server.properties\n    99→    ./bin/kafka-server-start.sh config/kraft/server.properties\n   100→\n   101→Using docker image:\n   102→\n   103→    docker run -p 9092:9092 apache/kafka:3.7.0\n   104→\n   105→### Running a Kafka broker in ZooKeeper mode\n   106→\n   107→Using compiled files:\n   108→\n   109→    ./bin/zookeeper-server-start.sh config/zookeeper.properties\n   110→    ./bin/kafka-server-start.sh config/server.properties\n   111→\n   112→>Since ZooKeeper mode is already deprecated and planned to be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0, the docker image only supports running in KRaft mode\n   113→\n   114→### Cleaning the build ###\n   115→    ./gradlew clean\n   116→\n   117→### Running a task with one of the Scala versions available (2.12.x or 2.13.x) ###\n   118→*Note that if building the jars with a version other than 2.13.x, you need to set the `SCALA_VERSION` variable or change it in `bin/kafka-run-class.sh` to run the quick start.*\n   119→\n   120→You can pass either the major version (eg 2.12) or the full version (eg 2.12.7):\n   121→\n   122→    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 jar\n   123→    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 test\n   124→    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 releaseTarGz\n   125→\n   126→### Running a task with all the scala versions enabled by default ###\n   127→\n   128→Invoke the `gradlewAll` script followed by the task(s):\n   129→\n   130→    ./gradlewAll test\n   131→    ./gradlewAll jar\n   132→    ./gradlewAll releaseTarGz\n   133→\n   134→### Running a task for a specific project ###\n   135→This is for `core`, `examples` and `clients`\n   136→\n   137→    ./gradlew core:jar\n   138→    ./gradlew core:test\n   139→\n   140→Streams has multiple sub-projects, but you can run all the tests:\n   141→\n   142→    ./gradlew :streams:testAll\n   143→\n   144→### Listing all gradle tasks Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.\n\n## Apache Kafka Codebase Analysis Report\n\n### 1. Root Directory Structure Overview\n\nThe Apache Kafka codebase (version 3.9.0) is organized as a Gradle multi-project build with the following main structure:\n\n**Core Directories:**\n- **core** - Legacy broker and server implementation (Scala/Java mix)\n- **server** - Modern server implementation (Java)\n- **server-common** - Common server utilities\n- **clients** - Producer and Consumer client libraries\n- **streams** - Kafka Streams topology processing library\n- **connect** - Kafka Connect framework with multiple sub-modules\n- **metadata** - Metadata and KRaft (Kafka Raft) implementation\n- **raft** - Raft consensus protocol implementation\n- **storage** - Storage layer abstraction\n- **group-coordinator** - Consumer group coordination\n- **transaction-coordinator** - Transaction coordination\n- **tools** - Command-line tools and utilities\n- **shell** - Interactive shell interface\n- **log4j-appender** - Logging integration\n- **jmh-benchmarks** - Performance benchmarks\n- **trogdor** - Workload generation and testing framework\n- **generator** - Code generation utilities\n- **examples** - Code examples\n- **tests** - Integration and system tests\n- **bin** - Shell scripts for running Kafka\n- **config** - Configuration templates\n- **docs** - Documentation\n- **docker** - Docker and container-related files\n\n**Build Configuration:**\n- **build.gradle** (3,564 lines) - Root Gradle build configuration\n- **settings.gradle** - Project structure definition with all modules\n- **gradle/** - Gradle configuration and dependency definitions\n- **gradlew** - Gradle wrapper for build execution\n\n---\n\n### 2. Core Modules Overview\n\n#### **Clients Module** (`/workspace/clients`)\n**Purpose:** Producer and consumer client implementations\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.clients.*` - Core client APIs\n- `org.apache.kafka.common.*` - Common utilities, configurations, protocols\n- `org.apache.kafka.server.*` - Server-side utilities\n\n**Key Functionality:**\n- Producer API for sending messages to Kafka topics\n- Consumer API for subscribing and consuming from topics\n- Metadata management and topic discovery\n- Network communication and protocol handling\n\n#### **Core Module** (`/workspace/core`)\n**Purpose:** Broker implementation, log management, and server infrastructure\n**Main Scala Packages:**\n- `kafka.Kafka` - Main entry point (Scala)\n- `kafka.server.*` - Broker server implementation (~500+ files)\n- `kafka.log.*` - Log storage and management (LogManager, UnifiedLog, LogCleaner)\n- `kafka.controller.*` - Cluster controller logic\n- `kafka.coordinator.*` - Coordination services\n- `kafka.cluster.*` - Cluster metadata\n- `kafka.network.*` - Network I/O handling\n- `kafka.metrics.*` - Metrics collection\n- `kafka.security.*` - Authentication and authorization\n- `kafka.utils.*` - Utility functions\n- `kafka.zk.*` - ZooKeeper integration (legacy)\n\n**Critical Classes:**\n- `KafkaServer` - Legacy ZooKeeper-based broker lifecycle\n- `KafkaRaftServer` - KRaft (Kafka Raft) mode broker implementation\n- `KafkaController` - Cluster controller state machine\n\n#### **Server Module** (`/workspace/server`)\n**Purpose:** Modern server infrastructure (Java-based)\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.server.*` - Common server components\n\n#### **Streams Module** (`/workspace/streams`)\n**Purpose:** Event streaming processing library\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.streams.*` - Topology, state stores, processing\n- Multiple sub-projects for examples and upgrade testing\n\n#### **Metadata Module** (`/workspace/metadata`)\n**Purpose:** KRaft metadata management and controller\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.metadata.*` - Metadata structures\n- `org.apache.kafka.controller.*` - Controller implementation\n- `org.apache.kafka.image.*` - Metadata snapshots and changelog\n\n#### **Raft Module** (`/workspace/raft`)\n**Purpose:** Raft consensus protocol implementation\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.raft.*` - Raft protocol and quorum management\n\n#### **Storage Module** (`/workspace/storage`)\n**Purpose:** Storage layer abstraction\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.storage.*` - Storage APIs and implementations\n\n#### **Group Coordinator Module** (`/workspace/group-coordinator`)\n**Purpose:** Consumer group coordination\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.coordinator.*` - Group coordination logic\n\n#### **Transaction Coordinator Module** (`/workspace/transaction-coordinator`)\n**Purpose:** Transactional message coordination\n**Implementation in core with this module for modular organization\n\n#### **Tools Module** (`/workspace/tools`)\n**Purpose:** CLI tools for Kafka administration and testing\n**Key Tools:**\n- TopicCommand - Create/modify/delete topics\n- ConsumerPerformance - Consumer benchmarking\n- ProducerPerformance - Producer benchmarking\n- StreamsResetter - Reset Kafka Streams applications\n- TransactionsCommand - Manage transactions\n- ReplicaVerificationTool - Verify replica data\n- MetadataQuorumCommand - KRaft quorum management\n- LeaderElectionCommand - Trigger leader elections\n- And 15+ more administrative and testing utilities\n\n#### **Connect Module** (`/workspace/connect`)\n**Purpose:** Kafka Connect distributed data integration framework\n**Sub-modules:**\n- `api` - Connect plugin API\n- `runtime` - Connect distributed/standalone runtime\n- `file` - File source/sink connectors\n- `json` - JSON data format support\n- `mirror` - MirrorMaker 2.0 implementation\n- `mirror-client` - Mirror client utilities\n- `basic-auth-extension` - Authentication extension\n- `transforms` - Built-in transformations\n- `test-plugins` - Test connector plugins\n\n#### **Shell Module** (`/workspace/shell`)\n**Purpose:** Interactive Kafka Shell interface\n\n#### **Trogdor Module** (`/workspace/trogdor`)\n**Purpose:** Workload generation and performance testing framework\n\n---\n\n### 3. Build System Details\n\n**Build Tool:** Gradle (with wrapper)\n- Root build file: `/workspace/build.gradle` (3,564 lines)\n- Gradle version: Managed via `gradle.properties`\n- Wrapper scripts: `gradlew`, `gradlewAll` (for multiple Scala versions)\n\n**Key Build Plugins:**\n- Java Library plugin\n- Checkstyle (code style)\n- SpotBugs (static analysis)\n- Jacoco (test coverage)\n- Maven Publish (artifact publishing)\n- Signing (GPG signing for releases)\n- Shadow (fat JAR creation for clients)\n- Spotless (code formatting - Java 11+)\n- Scoverage (Scala coverage)\n- Apache RAT (license verification)\n- Swagger (API documentation)\n\n**Supported Scala Versions:** 2.12 and 2.13 (2.13 default)\n**Supported Java Versions:** 8, 11, 17, 21\n- Java 8 support deprecated since 3.0, removal planned for 4.0\n- Java 11 support for broker deprecated since 3.7, removal planned for 4.0\n\n**Key Build Tasks:**\n- `./gradlew jar` - Build all JARs\n- `./gradlew test` - Run all tests\n- `./gradlew releaseTarGz` - Create binary release tarball\n- `./gradlew aggregatedJavadoc` - Generate API documentation\n\n---\n\n### 4. Kafka Broker Entry Point\n\n**Primary Entry Class:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/Kafka.scala`\n\n**Class:** `kafka.Kafka` (object/singleton)\n\n**Main Method Flow:**\n1. **Argument Parsing** - Loads server.properties configuration file with optional overrides\n   ```\n   Usage: java kafka.Kafka server.properties [--override property=value]*\n   ```\n\n2. **Server Selection** (based on `process.roles` config):\n   - **ZooKeeper Mode** (legacy): Instantiates `KafkaServer`\n   - **KRaft Mode** (modern): Instantiates `KafkaRaftServer`\n\n3. **Lifecycle Management:**\n   - Registers signal handlers for graceful shutdown\n   - Calls `server.startup()` to initialize broker\n   - Calls `server.awaitShutdown()` to wait for termination signal\n   - Calls `server.shutdown()` on termination\n\n**Related Classes:**\n- `kafka.server.KafkaServer` - Legacy ZooKeeper-based broker (lines: ~150+ at start with 400+ total imports)\n- `kafka.server.KafkaRaftServer` - Modern KRaft-based broker\n- `kafka.server.KafkaConfig` - Configuration management\n\n**Server Startup Orchestration:**\nThe KafkaServer class manages:\n- Socket server initialization (network listeners)\n- Log manager setup (log directories, recovery)\n- Replica manager (replication handling)\n- Controller (if applicable)\n- Metrics reporters\n- Request handlers (data plane and control plane)\n- Coordination services (groups, transactions)\n- Authorization and security\n\n---\n\n### 5. Key Configuration Files\n\n**Broker Configuration Files (in `/workspace/config`):**\n\n#### **server.properties** - ZooKeeper Mode Configuration\n- `broker.id` - Unique broker identifier\n- `listeners` - Network listener addresses\n- `advertised.listeners` - Client-facing listener addresses\n- `log.dirs` - Log directory paths\n- `num.partitions` - Default topic partitions\n- `num.network.threads` - Network thread count (default: 3)\n- `num.io.threads` - I/O thread count (default: 8)\n- `socket.send.buffer.bytes` - Socket send buffer (default: 102400)\n- `socket.receive.buffer.bytes` - Socket receive buffer (default: 102400)\n- `offsets.topic.replication.factor` - Consumer offset replication\n- `transaction.state.log.replication.factor` - Transaction log replication\n- **Note:** Requires ZooKeeper to be running separately\n\n#### **kraft/server.properties** - KRaft Mode Configuration\n- `process.roles` - Server role(s): `broker` and/or `controller`\n- `node.id` - Unique node identifier\n- `controller.quorum.voters` - Quorum voter addresses\n- `listeners` - PLAINTEXT and CONTROLLER listeners\n- `controller.listener.names` - Controller listener specification\n- `log.dirs` - Log directory paths\n- Default location: `/tmp/kraft-combined-logs`\n- **Note:** No ZooKeeper required; uses embedded Raft consensus\n\n#### **Other Configuration Files:**\n- `producer.properties` - Producer client defaults\n- `consumer.properties` - Consumer client defaults\n- `connect-*.properties` - Connect framework configurations\n- `zookeeper.properties` - ZooKeeper server configuration\n- `log4j.properties` - Logging configuration\n- `trogdor.conf` - Trogdor testing framework config\n\n---\n\n### 6. Architecture Summary\n\n**Kafka 3.9.0 uses a dual-mode architecture:**\n\n1. **Legacy Mode (ZooKeeper-based)**\n   - Relies on external Apache ZooKeeper cluster\n   - Used in production systems\n   - Deprecated but still supported\n   - Entry point: `KafkaServer` class\n\n2. **Modern Mode (KRaft - Kafka Raft)**\n   - Embedded Raft consensus for metadata management\n   - No external ZooKeeper dependency\n   - New deployments should use this mode\n   - Entry point: `KafkaRaftServer` class\n   - Components: Metadata module, Raft module, Controller\n\n**Core Components:**\n- **Network Layer** - Socket-based I/O with request/response handling\n- **Log Storage** - Partition-based log segments with log cleaner\n- **Replication** - Leader-follower replication with ISR (In-Sync Replicas)\n- **Coordination** - Consumer groups and transaction coordination\n- **Control Plane** - Leader election, partition assignment, state management\n- **Client Libraries** - Producer and Consumer APIs\n- **Stream Processing** - Kafka Streams for topology-based applications\n- **Data Integration** - Core Modules Overview\n\n#### **Clients Module** (`/workspace/clients`)\n**Purpose:** Producer and consumer client implementations\n**Packages:**\n- `org.apache.kafka.clients.*` - Core client APIs\n- `org.apache.kafka.common.*` - Common utilities, configurations, protocols\n- `org.apache.kafka.server.*` - Server-side utilities\n\n**Key Functionality:**\n- Producer API for sending messages to Kafka topics\n- Consumer API for subscribing and consuming from topics\n- Metadata management and topic discovery\n- Network communication and protocol handling\n\n#### **Core Module** (`/workspace/core`)\n**Purpose:** Broker implementation, log management, and server infrastructure\n**Main Scala Packages:**\n- `kafka.Kafka` - Coordination services\n- `kafka.cluster.*` - Cluster metadata\n- `kafka.network.*` - Network I/O handling\n- `kafka.metrics.*` - Metrics collection\n- `kafka.security.*` - Authentication and authorization\n- `kafka.utils.*` - Utility functions\n- `kafka.zk.*` - ZooKeeper integration (legacy)\n\n**Critical Classes:**\n- `KafkaServer` - Legacy ZooKeeper-based broker lifecycle\n- `KafkaRaftServer` - KRaft (Kafka Raft) mode broker implementation\n- `KafkaController` - Group coordination logic\n\n#### **Transaction Coordinator Module** (`/workspace/transaction-coordinator`)\n**Purpose:** Transactional message coordination\n**Implementation in core with this module for modular organization\n\n#### **Tools Module** (`/workspace/tools`)\n**Purpose:** CLI tools for Kafka administration and testing\n**Key Tools:**\n- TopicCommand - Manage transactions\n- ReplicaVerificationTool - Verify replica data\n- MetadataQuorumCommand - KRaft quorum management\n- LeaderElectionCommand - Trigger leader elections\n- And 15+ more administrative and testing utilities\n\n#### **Connect Module** (`/workspace/connect`)\n**Purpose:** Kafka Connect distributed data integration framework\n**Sub-modules:**\n- `api` - Connect plugin API\n- `runtime` - Connect distributed/standalone runtime\n- `file` - File source/sink connectors\n- `json` - JSON data format support\n- `mirror` - MirrorMaker 2.0 implementation\n- `mirror-client` - Mirror client utilities\n- `basic-auth-extension` - Authentication extension\n- `transforms` - Built-in transformations\n- `test-plugins` - Test connector plugins\n\n#### **Shell Module** (`/workspace/shell`)\n**Purpose:** Interactive Kafka Shell interface\n\n#### **Trogdor Module** (`/workspace/trogdor`)\n**Purpose:** Workload generation and performance testing framework\n\n---\n\n### 3. Build System Details\n\n**Build Tool:** Gradle (with wrapper)\n- Root build file: `/workspace/build.gradle` (3,564 lines)\n- Gradle version: Managed via `gradle.properties`\n- Wrapper scripts: `gradlew`, `gradlewAll` (for multiple Scala versions)\n\n**Key Build Plugins:**\n- Java Library plugin\n- Checkstyle (code style)\n- SpotBugs (static analysis)\n- Jacoco (test coverage)\n- Maven Publish (artifact publishing)\n- Signing (GPG signing for releases)\n- Shadow (fat JAR creation for clients)\n- Spotless (code formatting - Java 11+)\n- Scoverage (Scala coverage)\n- Apache RAT (license verification)\n- Swagger (API documentation)\n\n**Supported Scala Versions:** 2.12 and 2.13 (2.13 default)\n**Supported Java Versions:** 8, 11, 17, 21\n- Java 8 support deprecated since 3.0, removal planned for 4.0\n- Java 11 support for broker deprecated since 3.7, removal planned for 4.0\n\n**Key Build Tasks:**\n- `./gradlew jar` - Build all JARs\n- `./gradlew test` - Run all tests\n- `./gradlew releaseTarGz` - Create binary release tarball\n- `./gradlew aggregatedJavadoc` - Generate API documentation\n\n---\n\n### 4. Kafka Broker Entry Point\n\n**Primary Entry Class:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/Kafka.scala`\n\n**Class:** `kafka.Kafka` (object/singleton)\n\n**Main Method Flow:**\n1. **Argument Parsing** - Loads server.properties configuration file with optional overrides\n   ```\n   Usage: java kafka.Kafka server.properties [--override property=value]*\n   ```\n\n2. **Server Selection** (based on `process.roles` config):\n   - **ZooKeeper Mode** (legacy): Instantiates `KafkaServer`\n   - **KRaft Mode** (modern): Instantiates `KafkaRaftServer`\n\n3. **Lifecycle Management:**\n   - Registers signal handlers for graceful shutdown\n   - Calls `server.startup()` to initialize broker\n   - Calls `server.awaitShutdown()` to wait for termination signal\n   - Calls `server.shutdown()` on termination\n\n**Related Classes:**\n- `kafka.server.KafkaServer` - Legacy ZooKeeper-based broker (lines: ~150+ at start with 400+ total imports)\n- `kafka.server.KafkaRaftServer` - Modern KRaft-based broker\n- `kafka.server.KafkaConfig` - Configuration management\n\n**Server Startup Orchestration:**\nThe KafkaServer class manages:\n- Socket server initialization (network listeners)\n- Log manager setup (log directories, recovery)\n- Replica manager (replication handling)\n- Controller (if applicable)\n- Metrics reporters\n- Request handlers (data plane and control plane)\n- Coordination services (groups, transactions)\n- Authorization and security\n\n---\n\n### 5. Key Configuration Files\n\n**Broker Configuration Files (in `/workspace/config`):**\n\n#### **server.properties** - ZooKeeper Mode Configuration\n- `broker.id` - Unique broker identifier\n- `listeners` - Network listener addresses\n- `advertised.listeners` - Client-facing listener addresses\n- `log.dirs` - Log directory paths\n- `num.partitions` - Default topic partitions\n- `num.network.threads` - Network thread count (default: 3)\n- `num.io.threads` - I/O thread count (default: 8)\n- `socket.send.buffer.bytes` - Socket send buffer (default: 102400)\n- `socket.receive.buffer.bytes` - Socket receive buffer (default: 102400)\n- `offsets.topic.replication.factor` - Consumer offset replication\n- `transaction.state.log.replication.factor` - Transaction log replication\n- **Note:** Requires ZooKeeper to be running separately\n\n#### **kraft/server.properties** - KRaft Mode Configuration\n- `process.roles` - Server role(s): `broker` and/or `controller`\n- `node.id` - Unique node identifier\n- `controller.quorum.voters` - Quorum voter addresses\n- `listeners` - PLAINTEXT and CONTROLLER listeners\n- `controller.listener.names` - Controller listener specification\n- `log.dirs` - Log directory paths\n- Default location: `/tmp/kraft-combined-logs`\n- **Note:** No ZooKeeper required; uses embedded Raft consensus\n\n#### **Other Configuration Files:**\n- `producer.properties` - Producer client defaults\n- `consumer.properties` - Consumer client defaults\n- `connect-*.properties` - Connect framework configurations\n- `zookeeper.properties` - ZooKeeper server configuration\n- `log4j.properties` - Logging configuration\n- `trogdor.conf` - Trogdor testing framework config\n\n---\n\n### 6. Architecture Summary\n\n**Kafka 3.9.0 uses a dual-mode architecture:**\n\n1. **Legacy Mode (ZooKeeper-based)**\n   - Relies on external Apache ZooKeeper cluster\n   - Used in production systems\n   - Deprecated but still supported\n   - Entry point: `KafkaServer` class\n\n2. **Modern Mode (KRaft - Kafka Raft)**\n   - Embedded Raft consensus for metadata management\n   - No external ZooKeeper dependency\n   - New deployments should use this mode\n   - Entry point: `KafkaRaftServer` class\n   - Components: Metadata module, Raft module, Controller\n\n**Core Components:**\n- **Network Layer** - Socket-based I/O with request/response handling\n- **Log Storage** - Partition-based log segments with log cleaner\n- **Replication** - Leader-follower replication with ISR (In-Sync Replicas)\n- **Coordination** - Consumer groups and transaction coordination\n- **Control Plane** - Leader election, partition assignment, state management\n- **Client Libraries** - Producer and Consumer APIs\n- **Stream Processing** - Kafka Streams for topology-based applications\n- **Data Integration** - Kafka Connect for external system integration\n\n**Module Dependencies:**\n- Core depends on Clients, Metadata, Raft, Storage\n- Streams depends on Clients\n- Connect depends on Clients\n- Tools depends on Clients and Core\n\n---\n\n### Absolute File Paths for Key Locations\n\n1. **Main Broker Entry Point:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/Kafka.scala`\n2. **KafkaServer Class:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaServer.scala`\n3. **KafkaRaftServer Class:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaRaftServer.scala`\n4. **KafkaConfig Class:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`\n5. **ZooKeeper Configuration:** `/workspace/config/server.properties`\n6. **KRaft Configuration:** `/workspace/config/kraft/server.properties`\n7. **Build Configuration:** `/workspace/build.gradle`\n8. **Module Definition:** `/workspace/settings.gradle`\n9. **Tools Directory:** `/workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/`\n10. **Metadata Management:** `/workspace/metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/`\n\nThis comprehensive architecture supports Kafka's role as a distributed, highly-available message broker with strong consistency guarantees and multiple client integration patterns."}],"totalDurationMs":68940,"totalTokens":47648,"totalToolUseCount":61,"usage":{"input_tokens":5,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1100,"cache_read_input_tokens":43204,"output_tokens":3339,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1100,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

 71→import java.util.Optional;\n    72→import java.util.Properties;\n    73→import java.util.Set;\n    74→import java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException;\n    75→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    76→\n    77→import joptsimple.ArgumentAcceptingOptionSpec;\n    78→import joptsimple.OptionSpec;\n    79→import joptsimple.OptionSpecBuilder;\n    80→\n    81→public abstract class TopicCommand {\n    82→    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(TopicCommand.class);\n    83→\n    84→    public static void main(String... args) {\n    85→        Exit.exit(mainNoExit(args));\n    86→    }\n    87→\n    88→    private static int mainNoExit(String... args) {\n    89→        try {\n    90→            execute(args);\n    91→            return 0;\n    92→        } catch (Throwable e) {\n    93→            System.err.println(e.getMessage());\n    94→            System.err.println(Utils.stackTrace(e));\n    95→            return 1;\n    96→        }\n    97→    }\n    98→\n    99→    static void execute(String... args) throws Exception {\n   100→        TopicCommandOptions opts = new TopicCommandOptions(args);\n   101→        TopicService topicService = new TopicService(opts.commandConfig(), opts.bootstrapServer());\n   102→        int exitCode = 0;\n   103→        try {\n   104→            if (opts.hasCreateOption()) {\n   105→                topicService.createTopic(opts);\n   106→            } else if (opts.hasAlterOption()) {\n   107→                topicService.alterTopic(opts);\n   108→            } else if (opts.hasListOption()) {\n   109→                topicService.listTopics(opts);\n   110→            } else if (opts.hasDescribeOption()) {\n   111→                topicService.describeTopic(opts);\n   112→            } else if (opts.hasDeleteOption()) {\n   113→                topicService.deleteTopic(opts);\n   114→            }\n   115→        } catch (ExecutionException e) {\n   116→            Throwable cause = e.getCause();\n   117→            if (cause != null) {\n   118→                printException(cause);\n   119→            } else {\n   120→                printException(e);\n   121→            }\n   122→            exitCode = 1;\n   123→        } catch (Throwable e) {\n   124→            printException(e);\n   125→            exitCode = 1;\n   126→        } finally {\n   127→            topicService.close();\n   128→            Exit.exit(exitCode);\n   129→        }\n   130→    }\n   131→\n   132→    private static void printException(Throwable e) {\n   133→        System.out.println(\"Error while executing topic command : \" + e.getMessage());\n   134→        LOG.error(Utils.stackTrace(e));\n   135→    }\n   136→\n   137→    static Map<Integer, List<Integer>> parseReplicaAssignment(String replicaAssignmentList) {\n   138→        String[] partitionList = replicaAssignmentList.split(\",\");\n   139→        Map<Integer, List<Integer>> ret = new LinkedHashMap<>();\n   140→        for (int i = 0; i < partitionList.length; i++) {\n   141→            List<Integer> brokerList = Arrays.stream(partitionList[i].split(\":\"))\n   142→                .map(String::trim)\n   143→                .mapToInt(Integer::parseInt)\n   144→                .boxed()\n   145→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   146→            Collection<Integer> duplicateBrokers = ToolsUtils.duplicates(brokerList);\n   147→            if (!duplicateBrokers.isEmpty()) {\n   148→                throw new AdminCommandFailedException(\"Partition replica lists may not contain duplicate entries: \" +\n   149→                    duplicateBrokers.stream()\n   150→                        .map(Object::toString)\n   151→                        .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\"))\n   152→                );\n   153→            }\n   154→            ret.put(i, brokerList);\n   155→            if (ret.get(i).size() != ret.get(0).size()) {\n   156→                throw new AdminOperationException(\"Partition \" + i + \" has different replication factor: \" + brokerList);\n   157→            }\n   158→        }\n   159→        return ret;\n   160→    }\n   161→\n   162→    @SuppressWarnings(\"deprecation\")\n   163→    private static Properties parseTopicConfigsToBeAdded(TopicCommandOptions opts) {\n   164→        List<List<String>> configsToBeAdded = opts.topicConfig().orElse(Collections.emptyList())\n   165→            .stream()\n   166→            .map(s -> Arrays.asList(s.split(\"\\\\s*=\\\\s*\")))\n   167→            .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   168→\n   169→        if (!configsToBeAdded.stream().allMatch(config -> config.size() == 2)) {\n   170→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"requirement failed: Invalid topic config: all configs to be added must be in the format \\\"key=val\\\".\");\n   171→        }\n   172→\n   173→        Properties props = new Properties();\n   174→        configsToBeAdded.stream()\n   175→            .forEach(pair -> props.setProperty(pair.get(0).trim(), pair.get(1).trim()));\n   176→        LogConfig.validate(props);\n   177→        if (props.containsKey(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)) {\n   178→            System.out.println(\"WARNING: The configuration ${TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG}=${props.getProperty(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)} is specified. \" +\n   179→                \"This configuration will be ignored if the version is newer than the inter.broker.protocol.version specified in the broker or \" +\n   180→                \"if the inter.broker.protocol.version is 3.0 or newer. This configuration is deprecated and it will be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0.\");\n   181→        }\n   182→        return props;\n   183→    }\n   184→\n   185→    // It is possible for a reassignment to complete between the time we have fetched its state and the time\n   186→    // we fetch partition metadata. In this case, we ignore the reassignment when determining replication factor.\n   187→    public static boolean isReassignmentInProgress(TopicPartitionInfo tpi, PartitionReassignment ra) {\n   188→        // Reassignment is still in progress as long as the removing and adding replicas are still present\n   189→        Set<Integer> allReplicaIds = tpi.replicas().stream().map(Node::id).collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   190→        Set<Integer> changingReplicaIds = new HashSet<>();\n   191→        if (ra != null) {\n   192→            changingReplicaIds.addAll(ra.removingReplicas());\n   193→            changingReplicaIds.addAll(ra.addingReplicas());\n   194→        }\n   195→        return allReplicaIds.stream().anyMatch(changingReplicaIds::contains);\n   196→\n   197→    }\n   198→\n   199→    private static Integer getReplicationFactor(TopicPartitionInfo tpi, PartitionReassignment reassignment) {\n   200→        return isReassignmentInProgress(tpi, reassignment) ?\n   201→            reassignment.replicas().size() - reassignment.addingReplicas().size() :\n   202→            tpi.replicas().size();\n   203→    }\n   204→\n   205→    /**\n   206→     * ensures topic existence and throws exception if topic doesn't exist\n   207→     *\n   208→     * @param foundTopics        Topics that were found to match the requested topic name.\n   209→     * @param requestedTopic     Name of the topic that was requested.\n   210→     * @param requireTopicExists Indicates if the topic needs to exist for the operation to be successful.\n   211→     *                           If set to true, the command will throw an exception if the topic with the\n   212→     *                           requested name does not exist.\n   213→     */\n   214→    private static void ensureTopicExists(List<String> foundTopics, Optional<String> requestedTopic, Boolean requireTopicExists) {\n   215→        // If no topic name was mentioned, do not need to throw exception.\n   216→        if (requestedTopic.isPresent() && !requestedTopic.get().isEmpty() && requireTopicExists && foundTopics.isEmpty()) {\n   217→            // If given topic doesn't exist then throw exception\n   218→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(String.format(\"Topic '%s' does not exist as expected\", requestedTopic));\n   219→        }\n   220→    }\n   221→\n   222→    private static List<String> doGetTopics(List<String> allTopics, Optional<String> topicIncludeList, Boolean excludeInternalTopics) {\n   223→        if (topicIncludeList.isPresent()) {\n   224→            IncludeList topicsFilter = new IncludeList(topicIncludeList.get());\n   225→            return allTopics.stream()\n   226→                .filter(topic -> topicsFilter.isTopicAllowed(topic, excludeInternalTopics))\n   227→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   228→        } else {\n   229→            return allTopics.stream()\n   230→                .filter(topic -> !(Topic.isInternal(topic) && excludeInternalTopics))\n   231→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   232→        }\n   233→    }\n   234→\n   235→    /**\n   236→     * ensures topic existence and throws exception if topic doesn't exist\n   237→     *\n   238→     * @param foundTopicIds        Topics that were found to match the requested topic id.\n   239→     * @param requestedTopicId     Id of the topic that was requested.\n   240→     * @param requireTopicIdExists Indicates if the topic needs to exist for the operation to be successful.\n   241→     *                             If set to true, the command will throw an exception if the topic with the\n   242→     *                             requested id does not exist.\n   243→     */\n   244→    private static void ensureTopicIdExists(List<Uuid> foundTopicIds, Uuid requestedTopicId, Boolean requireTopicIdExists) {\n   245→        // If no topic id was mentioned, do not need to throw exception.\n   246→        if (requestedTopicId != null && requireTopicIdExists && foundTopicIds.isEmpty()) {\n   247→            // If given topicId doesn't exist then throw exception\n   248→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(String.format(\"TopicId '%s' does not exist as expected\", requestedTopicId));\n   249→        }\n   250→    }\n   251→\n   252→    static class CommandTopicPartition {\n   253→        private final String name;\n   254→        private final Optional<Integer> partitions;\n   255→        private final Optional<Integer> replicationFactor;\n   256→        private final Map<Integer, List<Integer>> replicaAssignment;\n   257→        private final Properties configsToAdd;\n   258→\n   259→        private final TopicCommandOptions opts;\n   260→\n   261→        public CommandTopicPartition(TopicCommandOptions options) {\n   262→            opts = options;\n   263→            name = options.topic().get();\n   264→            partitions = options.partitions();\n   265→            replicationFactor = options.replicationFactor();\n   266→            replicaAssignment = options.replicaAssignment().orElse(Collections.emptyMap());\n   267→            configsToAdd = parseTopicConfigsToBeAdded(options);\n   268→        }\n   269→\n   270→        public Boolean hasReplicaAssignment() {\n   271→            return !replicaAssignment.isEmpty();\n   272→        }\n   273→\n   274→        public Boolean ifTopicDoesntExist() {\n   275→            return opts.ifNotExists();\n   276→        }\n   277→    }\n   278→\n   279→    static class TopicDescription {\n   280→        private final String topic;\n   281→        private final Uuid topicId;\n   282→        private final Integer numPartitions;\n   283→        private final Integer replicationFactor;\n   284→        private final Config config;\n   285→        private final Boolean markedForDeletion;\n   286→\n   287→        public TopicDescription(String topic, Uuid topicId, Integer numPartitions, Integer replicationFactor, Config config, Boolean markedForDeletion) {\n   288→            this.topic = topic;\n   289→            this.topicId = topicId;\n   290→            this.numPartitions = numPartitions;\n   291→            this.replicationFactor = replicationFactor;\n   292→            this.config = config;\n   293→            this.markedForDeletion = markedForDeletion;\n   294→        }\n   295→\n   296→        public void printDescription() {\n   297→            String configsAsString = config.entries().stream()\n   298→                .filter(config -> !config.isDefault())\n   299→                .map(ce -> ce.name() + \"=\" + ce.value())\n   300→                .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\"));\n   301→            System.out.print(\"Topic: \" +  topic);\n   302→            if (!topicId.equals(Uuid.ZERO_UUID))\n   303→                System.out.print(\"\\tTopicId: \" + topicId);\n   304→            System.out.print(\"\\tPartitionCount: \" + numPartitions);\n   305→            System.out.print(\"\\tReplicationFactor: \" + replicationFactor);\n   306→            System.out.print(\"\\tConfigs: \" + configsAsString);\n   307→            System.out.print(markedForDeletion ? \"\\tMarkedForDeletion: true\" : \"\");\n   308→            System.out.println();\n   309→        }\n   310→    }\n   311→\n   312→    static class PartitionDescription {\n   313→        private final String topic;\n   314→        private final TopicPartitionInfo info;\n   315→        private final Config config;\n   316→        private final Boolean markedForDeletion;\n   317→        private final PartitionReassignment reassignment;\n   318→\n   319→        PartitionDescription(String topic,\n   320→                             TopicPartitionInfo info,\n   321→                             Config config,\n   322→                             Boolean markedForDeletion,\n   323→                             PartitionReassignment reassignment) {\n   324→            this.topic = topic;\n   325→            this.info = info;\n   326→            this.config = config;\n   327→            this.markedForDeletion = markedForDeletion;\n   328→            this.reassignment = reassignment;\n   329→        }\n   330→\n   331→        public Integer minIsrCount() {\n   332→            return Integer.parseInt(config.get(TopicConfig.MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG).value());\n   333→        }\n   334→\n   335→        public Boolean isUnderReplicated() {\n   336→            return getReplicationFactor(info, reassignment) - info.isr().size() > 0;\n   337→        }\n   338→\n   339→        public boolean hasLeader() {\n   340→            return info.leader() != null;\n   341→        }\n   342→\n   343→        public Boolean isUnderMinIsr() {\n   344→            return !hasLeader() ||  info.isr().size() < minIsrCount();\n   345→        }\n   346→\n   347→        public Boolean isAtMinIsrPartitions() {\n   348→            return minIsrCount() == info.isr().size();\n   349→        }\n   350→\n   351→        public Boolean hasUnavailablePartitions(Set<Integer> liveBrokers) {\n   352→            return !hasLeader() || !liveBrokers.contains(info.leader().id());\n   353→        }\n   354→\n   355→        public void printDescription() {\n   356→            System.out.print(\"\\tTopic: \" + topic);\n   357→            System.out.print(\"\\tPartition: \" + info.partition());\n   358→            System.out.print(\"\\tLeader: \" + (hasLeader() ? info.leader().id() : \"none\"));\n   359→            System.out.print(\"\\tReplicas: \" + info.replicas().stream()\n   360→                .map(node -> Integer.toString(node.id()))\n   361→                .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   362→            System.out.print(\"\\tIsr: \" + info.isr().stream()\n   363→                .map(node -> Integer.toString(node.id()))\n   364→                .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   365→            if (reassignment != null) {\n   366→                System.out.print(\"\\tAdding Replicas: \" + reassignment.addingReplicas().stream()\n   367→                    .map(node -> node.toString())\n   368→                    .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   369→                System.out.print(\"\\tRemoving Replicas: \" + reassignment.removingReplicas().stream()\n   370→                    .map(node -> node.toString())\n   371→                    .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   372→            }\n   373→\n   374→            if (info.elr() != null) {\n   375→                System.out.print(\"\\tElr: \" + info.elr().stream()\n   376→                    .map(node -> Integer.toString(node.id()))\n   377→                    .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   378→            } else {\n   379→                System.out.print(\"\\tElr: N/A\");\n   380→            }\n   381→\n   382→            if (info.lastKnownElr() != null) {\n   383→                System.out.print(\"\\tLastKnownElr: \" + info.lastKnownElr().stream()\n   384→                    .map(node -> Integer.toString(node.id()))\n   385→                    .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\")));\n   386→            } else {\n   387→                System.out.print(\"\\tLastKnownElr: N/A\");\n   388→            }\n   389→            System.out.print(markedForDeletion ? \"\\tMarkedForDeletion: true\" : \"\");\n   390→            System.out.println();\n   391→        }\n   392→    }\n   393→\n   394→    static class DescribeOptions {\n   395→        private final TopicCommandOptions opts;\n   396→        private final Set<Integer> liveBrokers;\n   397→        private final boolean describeConfigs;\n   398→        private final boolean describePartitions;\n   399→\n   400→        public DescribeOptions(TopicCommandOptions opts, Set<Integer> liveBrokers) {\n   401→            this.opts = opts;\n   402→            this.liveBrokers = liveBrokers;\n   403→            this.describeConfigs = !opts.reportUnavailablePartitions() &&\n   404→                !opts.reportUnderReplicatedPartitions() &&\n   405→                !opts.reportUnderMinIsrPartitions() &&\n   406→                !opts.reportAtMinIsrPartitions();\n   407→            this.describePartitions = !opts.reportOverriddenConfigs();\n   408→        }\n   409→\n   410→        private boolean shouldPrintUnderReplicatedPartitions(PartitionDescription partitionDescription) {\n   411→            return opts.reportUnderReplicatedPartitions() && partitionDescription.isUnderReplicated();\n   412→        }\n   413→\n   414→        private boolean shouldPrintUnavailablePartitions(PartitionDescription partitionDescription) {\n   415→            return opts.reportUnavailablePartitions() && partitionDescription.hasUnavailablePartitions(liveBrokers);\n   416→        }\n   417→\n   418→        private boolean shouldPrintUnderMinIsrPartitions(PartitionDescription partitionDescription) {\n   419→            return opts.reportUnderMinIsrPartitions() && partitionDescription.isUnderMinIsr();\n   420→        }\n   421→\n   422→        private boolean shouldPrintAtMinIsrPartitions(PartitionDescription partitionDescription) {\n   423→            return opts.reportAtMinIsrPartitions() && partitionDescription.isAtMinIsrPartitions();\n   424→        }\n   425→\n   426→        private boolean shouldPrintTopicPartition(PartitionDescription partitionDesc) {\n   427→            return describeConfigs ||\n   428→                shouldPrintUnderReplicatedPartitions(partitionDesc) ||\n   429→                shouldPrintUnavailablePartitions(partitionDesc) ||\n   430→                shouldPrintUnderMinIsrPartitions(partitionDesc) ||\n   431→                shouldPrintAtMinIsrPartitions(partitionDesc);\n   432→        }\n   433→\n   434→        public void maybePrintPartitionDescription(PartitionDescription desc) {\n   435→            if (shouldPrintTopicPartition(desc)) {\n   436→                desc.printDescription();\n   437→            }\n   438→        }\n   439→    }\n   440→\n   441→    public static class TopicService implements AutoCloseable {\n   442→        private final Admin adminClient;\n   443→\n   444→        public TopicService(Properties commandConfig, Optional<String> bootstrapServer) {\n   445→            this.adminClient = createAdminClient(commandConfig, bootstrapServer);\n   446→        }\n   447→\n   448→        public TopicService(Admin admin) {\n   449→            this.adminClient = admin;\n   450→        }\n   451→\n   452→        private static Admin createAdminClient(Properties commandConfig, Optional<String> bootstrapServer) {\n   453→            if (bootstrapServer.isPresent()) {\n   454→                commandConfig.put(AdminClientConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServer.get());\n   455→            }\n   456→            return Admin.create(commandConfig);\n   457→        }\n   458→\n   459→        public void createTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws Exception {\n   460→            CommandTopicPartition topic = new CommandTopicPartition(opts);\n   461→            if (Topic.hasCollisionChars(topic.name)) {\n   462→                System.out.println(\"WARNING: Due to limitations in metric names, topics with a period ('.') or underscore ('_') could \" +\n   463→                    \"collide. To avoid issues it is best to use either, but not both.\");\n   464→            }\n   465→            createTopic(topic);\n   466→        }\n   467→\n   468→        public void createTopic(CommandTopicPartition topic) throws Exception {\n   469→            if (topic.replicationFactor.filter(rf -> rf > Short.MAX_VALUE || rf < 1).isPresent()) {\n   470→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The replication factor must be between 1 and \" + Short.MAX_VALUE + \" inclusive\");\n   471→            }\n   472→            if (topic.partitions.filter(p -> p < 1).isPresent()) {\n   473→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The partitions must be greater than 0\");\n   474→            }\n   475→\n   476→            try {\n   477→                NewTopic newTopic;\n   478→                if (topic.hasReplicaAssignment()) {\n   479→                    newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.replicaAssignment);\n   480→                } else {\n   481→                    newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.partitions, topic.replicationFactor.map(Integer::shortValue));\n   482→                }\n   483→\n   484→                Map<String, String> configsMap = topic.configsToAdd.stringPropertyNames().stream()\n   485→                    .collect(Collectors.toMap(name -> name, name -> topic.configsToAdd.getProperty(name)));\n   486→\n   487→                newTopic.configs(configsMap);\n   488→                CreateTopicsResult createResult = adminClient.createTopics(Collections.singleton(newTopic),\n   489→                    new CreateTopicsOptions().retryOnQuotaViolation(false));\n   490→                createResult.all().get();\n   491→                System.out.println(\"Created topic \" + topic.name + \".\");\n   492→            } catch (ExecutionException e) {\n   493→                if (e.getCause() == null) {\n   494→                    throw e;\n   495→                }\n   496→                if (!(e.getCause() instanceof TopicExistsException && topic.ifTopicDoesntExist())) {\n   497→                    throw (Exception) e.getCause();\n   498→                }\n   499→            }\n   500→        }\n   501→\n   502→        public void listTopics(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   503→            String results = getTopics(opts.topic(), opts.excludeInternalTopics())\n   504→                .stream()\n   505→                .collect(Collectors.joining(\"\\n\"));\n   506→            System.out.println(results);\n   507→        }\n   508→\n   509→        public void alterTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   510→            CommandTopicPartition topic = new CommandTopicPartition(opts);\n   511→            List<String> topics = getTopics(opts.topic(), opts.excludeInternalTopics());\n   512→            ensureTopicExists(topics, opts.topic(), !opts.ifExists());\n   513→\n   514→            if (!topics.isEmpty()) {\n   515→                Map<String, KafkaFuture<org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription>> topicsInfo = adminClient.describeTopics(topics).topicNameValues();\n   516→                Map<String, NewPartitions> newPartitions = topics.stream()\n   517→                    .map(topicName -> topicNewPartitions(topic, topicsInfo, topicName))\n   518→                    .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   519→                adminClient.createPartitions(newPartitions, new CreatePartitionsOptions().retryOnQuotaViolation(false)).all().get();\n   520→            }\n   521→        }\n   522→\n   523→        private AbstractMap.SimpleEntry<String, NewPartitions> topicNewPartitions(\n   524→            CommandTopicPartition topic,\n   525→            Map<String, KafkaFuture<org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription>> topicsInfo,\n   526→            String topicName) {\n   527→            if (topic.hasReplicaAssignment()) {\n   528→                try {\n   529→                    Integer startPartitionId = topicsInfo.get(topicName).get().partitions().size();\n   530→                    Map<Integer, List<Integer>> replicaMap = topic.replicaAssignment.entrySet().stream()\n   531→                        .skip(startPartitionId)\n   532→                        .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   533→                    List<List<Integer>> newAssignment = new ArrayList<>(replicaMap.values());\n   534→                    return new AbstractMap.SimpleEntry<>(topicName, NewPartitions.increaseTo(topic.partitions.get(), newAssignment));\n   535→                } catch (InterruptedException | ExecutionException e) {\n   536→                    throw new RuntimeException(e);\n   537→                }\n   538→            }\n   539→            return new AbstractMap.SimpleEntry<>(topicName, NewPartitions.increaseTo(topic.partitions.get()));\n   540→        }\n   541→\n   542→        public Map<TopicPartition, PartitionReassignment> listAllReassignments(Set<TopicPartition> topicPartitions) {\n   543→            try {\n   544→                return adminClient.listPartitionReassignments(topicPartitions).reassignments().get();\n   545→            } catch (ExecutionException e) {\n   546→                Throwable cause = e.getCause();\n   547→                if (cause instanceof UnsupportedVersionException || cause instanceof ClusterAuthorizationException) {\n   548→                    LOG.debug(\"Couldn't query reassignments through the AdminClient API: \" + cause.getMessage(), cause);\n   549→                    return Collections.emptyMap();\n   550→                } else {\n   551→                    throw new RuntimeException(e);\n   552→                }\n   553→            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n   554→                throw new RuntimeException(e);\n   555→            }\n   556→        }\n   557→\n   558→        public void describeTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   559→            // If topicId is provided and not zero, will use topicId regardless of topic name\n   560→            Optional<Uuid> inputTopicId = opts.topicId()\n   561→                .map(Uuid::fromString).filter(uuid -> !uuid.equals(Uuid.ZERO_UUID));\n   562→            Boolean useTopicId = inputTopicId.isPresent();\n   563→\n   564→            List<Uuid> topicIds;\n   565→            List<String> topics;\n   566→            if (useTopicId) {\n   567→                topicIds = getTopicIds(inputTopicId.get(), opts.excludeInternalTopics());\n   568→                topics = Collections.emptyList();\n   569→            } else {\n   570→                topicIds = Collections.emptyList();\n   571→                topics = getTopics(opts.topic(), opts.excludeInternalTopics());\n   572→            }\n   573→\n   574→            // Only check topic name when topicId is not provided\n   575→            if (useTopicId) {\n   576→                ensureTopicIdExists(topicIds, inputTopicId.get(), !opts.ifExists());\n   577→            } else {\n   578→                ensureTopicExists(topics, opts.topic(), !opts.ifExists());\n   579→            }\n   580→            List<org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription> topicDescriptions = new ArrayList<>();\n   581→\n   582→            if (!topicIds.isEmpty()) {\n   583→                Map<Uuid, org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription> descTopics =\n   584→                    adminClient.describeTopics(TopicCollection.ofTopicIds(topicIds)).allTopicIds().get();\n   585→                topicDescriptions = new ArrayList<>(descTopics.values());\n   586→            }\n   587→\n   588→            if (!topics.isEmpty()) {\n   589→                Map<String, org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription> descTopics =\n   590→                    adminClient.describeTopics(TopicCollection.ofTopicNames(topics),\n   591→                        new DescribeTopicsOptions()\n   592→                            .partitionSizeLimitPerResponse(opts.partitionSizeLimitPerResponse().orElse(2000))).allTopicNames().get();\n   593→                topicDescriptions = new ArrayList<>(descTopics.values());\n   594→            }\n   595→\n   596→            List<String> topicNames = topicDescriptions.stream()\n   597→                .map(org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription::name)\n   598→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   599→            Map<ConfigResource, KafkaFuture<Config>> allConfigs = adminClient.describeConfigs(\n   600→                topicNames.stream()\n   601→                    .map(name -> new ConfigResource(ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC, name))\n   602→                    .collect(Collectors.toList())\n   603→            ).values();\n   604→            List<Integer> liveBrokers = adminClient.describeCluster().nodes().get().stream()\n   605→                .map(Node::id)\n   606→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   607→            DescribeOptions describeOptions = new DescribeOptions(opts, new HashSet<>(liveBrokers));\n   608→            Set<TopicPartition> topicPartitions = topicDescriptions\n   609→                .stream()\n   610→                .flatMap(td -> td.partitions().stream()\n   611→                    .map(p -> new TopicPartition(td.name(), p.partition())))\n   612→                .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   613→            Map<TopicPartition, PartitionReassignment> reassignments = listAllReassignments(topicPartitions);\n   614→            for (org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription td : topicDescriptions) {\n   615→                String topicName = td.name();\n   616→                Uuid topicId = td.topicId();\n   617→                Config config = allConfigs.get(new ConfigResource(ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC, topicName)).get();\n   618→                ArrayList<TopicPartitionInfo> sortedPartitions = new ArrayList<>(td.partitions());\n   619→                sortedPartitions.sort(Comparator.comparingInt(TopicPartitionInfo::partition));\n   620→                printDescribeConfig(opts, describeOptions, reassignments, td, topicName, topicId, config, sortedPartitions);\n   621→                printPartitionDescription(describeOptions, reassignments, td, topicName, config, sortedPartitions);\n   622→            }\n   623→        }\n   624→\n   625→        private void printPartitionDescription(DescribeOptions describeOptions, Map<TopicPartition, PartitionReassignment> reassignments, org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription td, String topicName, Config config, ArrayList<TopicPartitionInfo> sortedPartitions) {\n   626→            if (describeOptions.describePartitions) {\n   627→                for (TopicPartitionInfo partition : sortedPartitions) {\n   628→                    PartitionReassignment reassignment =\n   629→                        reassignments.get(new TopicPartition(td.name(), partition.partition()));\n   630→                    PartitionDescription partitionDesc = new PartitionDescription(topicName,\n   631→                        partition, config, false, reassignment);\n   632→                    describeOptions.maybePrintPartitionDescription(partitionDesc);\n   633→                }\n   634→            }\n   635→        }\n   636→\n   637→        private void printDescribeConfig(TopicCommandOptions opts, DescribeOptions describeOptions, Map<TopicPartition, PartitionReassignment> reassignments, org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.TopicDescription td, String topicName, Uuid topicId, Config config, ArrayList<TopicPartitionInfo> sortedPartitions) {\n   638→            if (describeOptions.describeConfigs) {\n   639→                List<ConfigEntry> entries = new ArrayList<>(config.entries());\n   640→                boolean hasNonDefault = entries.stream().anyMatch(e -> !e.isDefault());\n   641→                if (!opts.reportOverriddenConfigs() || hasNonDefault) {\n   642→                    int numPartitions = td.partitions().size();\n   643→                    TopicPartitionInfo firstPartition = sortedPartitions.get(0);\n   644→                    PartitionReassignment reassignment =\n   645→                        reassignments.get(new TopicPartition(td.name(), firstPartition.partition()));\n   646→                    TopicDescription topicDesc = new TopicDescription(topicName, topicId,\n   647→                        numPartitions, getReplicationFactor(firstPartition, reassignment),\n   648→                        config, false);\n   649→                    topicDesc.printDescription();\n   650→                }\n   651→            }\n   652→        }\n   653→\n   654→        public void deleteTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   655→            List<String> topics = getTopics(opts.topic(), opts.excludeInternalTopics());\n   656→            ensureTopicExists(topics, opts.topic(), !opts.ifExists());\n   657→            adminClient.deleteTopics(Collections.unmodifiableList(topics),\n   658→                new DeleteTopicsOptions().retryOnQuotaViolation(false)\n   659→            ).all().get();\n   660→        }\n   661→\n   662→        public List<String> getTopics(Optional<String> topicIncludeList, boolean excludeInternalTopics) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   663→            ListTopicsOptions listTopicsOptions = new ListTopicsOptions();\n   664→            if (!excludeInternalTopics) {\n   665→                listTopicsOptions.listInternal(true);\n   666→            }\n   667→\n   668→            Set<String> allTopics = adminClient.listTopics(listTopicsOptions).names().get();\n   669→            return doGetTopics(allTopics.stream().sorted().collect(Collectors.toList()), topicIncludeList, excludeInternalTopics);\n   670→        }\n   671→\n   672→        public List<Uuid> getTopicIds(Uuid topicIdIncludeList, boolean excludeInternalTopics) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException {\n   673→            ListTopicsResult allTopics = excludeInternalTopics ? adminClient.listTopics() :\n   674→                adminClient.listTopics(new ListTopicsOptions().listInternal(true));\n   675→            List<Uuid> allTopicIds = allTopics.listings().get().stream()\n   676→                .map(TopicListing::topicId)\n   677→                .sorted()\n   678→                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   679→            return allTopicIds.contains(topicIdIncludeList) ?\n   680→                Collections.singletonList(topicIdIncludeList) :\n   681→                Collections.emptyList();\n   682→        }\n   683→\n   684→        @Override\n   685→        public void close()
