Here's the comprehensive breakdown from client to broker to storage:\n\n### 1. CLIENT-SIDE TOPIC CREATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/TopicCommand.java`\n\nThe client-side entry point uses the `TopicService` class which uses the Kafka Admin API:\n\n```java\npublic void createTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws Exception {\n    CommandTopicPartition topic = new CommandTopicPartition(opts);\n    createTopic(topic);\n}\n\npublic void createTopic(CommandTopicPartition topic) throws Exception {\n    NewTopic newTopic;\n    if (topic.hasReplicaAssignment()) {\n        newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.replicaAssignment);\n    } else {\n        newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.partitions, \n            topic.replicationFactor.map(Integer::shortValue));\n    }\n    \n    Map<String, String> configsMap = topic.configsToAdd.stringPropertyNames()\n        .stream()\n        .collect(Collectors.toMap(name -> name, \n            name -> topic.configsToAdd.getProperty(name)));\n    \n    newTopic.configs(configsMap);\n    CreateTopicsResult createResult = adminClient.createTopics(\n        Collections.singleton(newTopic),\n        new CreateTopicsOptions().retryOnQuotaViolation(false));\n    createResult.all().get();\n    System.out.println(\"Created topic \" + topic.name + \".\");\n}\n```\n\nKey classes:\n- `NewTopic` - encapsulates topic configuration\n- `CreateTopicsOptions` - options for creation (quota violation handling)\n- `Admin` client - handles communication with broker\n\n---\n\n### 2. REQUEST/RESPONSE PROTOCOL\n\n**Request:** `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/CreateTopicsRequest.java`\n\n```java\npublic class CreateTopicsRequest extends AbstractRequest {\n    private final CreateTopicsRequestData data;\n    \n    public CreateTopicsRequest(CreateTopicsRequestData data, short version) {\n        super(ApiKeys.CREATE_TOPICS, version);\n        this.data = data;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Response:** `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/CreateTopicsResponse.java`\n\nPossible error codes:\n- `REQUEST_TIMED_OUT(7)`\n- `INVALID_TOPIC_EXCEPTION(17)`\n- `TOPIC_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED(29)`\n- `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS(36)`\n- `INVALID_PARTITIONS(37)`\n- `INVALID_REPLICATION_FACTOR(38)`\n- `INVALID_REPLICA_ASSIGNMENT(39)`\n- `INVALID_CONFIG(40)`\n- `NOT_CONTROLLER(41)`\n\n---\n\n### 3. BROKER-SIDE REQUEST HANDLING\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaApis.scala`\n\nThe broker routes CREATE_TOPICS requests with special handling:\n\n```scala\ncase ApiKeys.CREATE_TOPICS => \n    maybeForwardToController(request, handleCreateTopicsRequest)\n```\n\n**Handler:** `handleCreateTopicsRequest` (line 2002-2098)\n\nThe flow:\n1. Checks if controller is active\n2. Validates authorization (CLUSTER or TOPIC CREATE permission)\n3. Filters out internal topics (e.g., `__cluster_metadata`)\n4. Calls `adminManager.createTopics()` with metadata callback\n\n```scala\ndef handleCreateTopicsRequest(request: RequestChannel.Request): Unit = {\n    val zkSupport = metadataSupport.requireZkOrThrow(...)\n    val controllerMutationQuota = quotas.controllerMutation.newQuotaFor(...)\n    \n    // ... validation checks ...\n    \n    zkSupport.adminManager.createTopics(\n        createTopicsRequest.data.timeoutMs,\n        createTopicsRequest.data.validateOnly,\n        toCreate,\n        authorizedForDescribeConfigs,\n        controllerMutationQuota,\n        handleCreateTopicsResults)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. ADMIN MANAGER - TOPIC CREATION ORCHESTRATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ZkAdminManager.scala`\n\nThe `createTopics` method (lines 159-258) performs the core topic creation logic:\n\n```scala\ndef createTopics(timeout: Int,\n                 validateOnly: Boolean,\n                 toCreate: Map[String, CreatableTopic],\n                 includeConfigsAndMetadata: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n                 controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n                 responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit): Unit = {\n\n    // 1. Validate and prepare metadata for each topic\n    val brokers = metadataCache.getAliveBrokers()\n    val metadata = toCreate.values.map(topic => {\n        // Check topic doesn't already exist\n        if (metadataCache.contains(topic.name))\n            throw new TopicExistsException(...)\n        \n        // Resolve replica assignments (auto or manual)\n        val assignments = if (topic.assignments.isEmpty) {\n            // Auto-assign replicas to brokers\n            AdminUtils.assignReplicasToBrokers(\n                brokers, resolvedNumPartitions, resolvedReplicationFactor)\n        } else {\n            // Use provided manual assignments\n            topic.assignments.forEach { assignment =>\n                assignments(assignment.partitionIndex) = \n                    assignment.brokerIds.asScala.map(a => a: Int)\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Validate assignment and configuration\n        adminZkClient.validateTopicCreate(topic.name, assignments, configs)\n        validateTopicCreatePolicy(...)\n        \n        // Populate metadata and configs for response\n        maybePopulateMetadataAndConfigs(..., topic.name, configs, assignments)\n        \n        if (validateOnly) {\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        } else {\n            // Write to ZooKeeper\n            controllerMutationQuota.record(assignments.size)\n            adminZkClient.createTopicWithAssignment(\n                topic.name, configs, assignments, validate = false)\n            populateIds(...)\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        }\n    }).toBuffer\n    \n    // 2. Handle immediate responses or delayed operations\n    if (timeout <= 0 || validateOnly || !metadata.exists(...)) {\n        // Immediate response\n        responseCallback(results)\n    } else {\n        // 3. Create delayed operation and watch for partition leaders\n        val delayedCreate = new DelayedCreatePartitions(\n            timeout, metadata, this, responseCallback)\n        val delayedCreateKeys = toCreate.values.map(topic => TopicKey(topic.name))\n        topicPurgatory.tryCompleteElseWatch(delayedCreate, delayedCreateKeys)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key responsibilities:**\n- Validate topic doesn't exist\n- Calculate replica assignments (or use provided ones)\n- Write topic configs to ZooKeeper\n- Create partition assignments in ZooKeeper\n- Queue delayed operation to wait for leader election\n\n---\n\n### 5. ZOOKEEPER WRITES\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/zk/AdminZkClient.scala`\n\n```scala\ndef createTopicWithAssignment(topic: String,\n                              config: Properties,\n                              partitionReplicaAssignment: Map[Int, Seq[Int]],\n                              validate: Boolean = true,\n                              usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    \n    if (validate)\n        validateTopicCreate(topic, partitionReplicaAssignment, config)\n    \n    // Write topic configuration\n    zkClient.setOrCreateEntityConfigs(ConfigType.TOPIC, topic, config)\n    \n    // Create partition assignment in ZooKeeper\n    writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic, \n        partitionReplicaAssignment.map { case (k, v) => \n            k -> ReplicaAssignment(v) \n        },\n        isUpdate = false, \n        usesTopicId)\n}\n\nprivate def writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic: String, \n                                          replicaAssignment: Map[Int, ReplicaAssignment],\n                                          isUpdate: Boolean, \n                                          usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    val assignment = replicaAssignment.map { \n        case (partitionId, replicas) => \n            (new TopicPartition(topic, partitionId), replicas) \n    }.toMap\n    \n    if (!isUpdate) {\n        val topicIdOpt = if (usesTopicId) Some(Uuid.randomUuid()) else None\n        zkClient.createTopicAssignment(topic, topicIdOpt, \n            assignment.map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**ZooKeeper paths created:**\n- `/config/topics/{topic-name}` - topic configuration\n- `/brokers/topics/{topic-name}` - partition assignment\n- Topic ID is stored as part of the assignment\n\n---\n\n### 6. DELAYED OPERATION - WAITING FOR LEADERS\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DelayedCreatePartitions.scala`\n\nOnce the metadata is written to ZooKeeper, a `DelayedCreatePartitions` operation waits for all partition leaders to be elected:\n\n```scala\nclass DelayedCreatePartitions(delayMs: Long,\n                              createMetadata: Seq[CreatePartitionsMetadata],\n                              adminManager: ZkAdminManager,\n                              responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit)\n  extends DelayedOperation(delayMs) {\n  \n  override def tryComplete(): Boolean = {\n    val leaderlessPartitionCount = createMetadata\n        .filter(_.error.isSuccess)\n        .foldLeft(0) { case (topicCounter, metadata) =>\n            topicCounter + missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions)\n        }\n    \n    if (leaderlessPartitionCount == 0) {\n        trace(\"All partitions have a leader, completing the delayed operation\")\n        forceComplete()\n    } else {\n        false\n    }\n  }\n  \n  override def onComplete(): Unit = {\n    val results = createMetadata.map { metadata =>\n        if (metadata.error.isSuccess && \n            missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions) > 0)\n            (metadata.topic, new ApiError(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, null))\n        else\n            (metadata.topic, metadata.error)\n    }.toMap\n    responseCallback(results)\n  }\n  \n  private def isMissingLeader(topic: String, partition: Int): Boolean = {\n    val partitionInfo = adminManager.metadataCache.getPartitionInfo(topic, partition)\n    partitionInfo.forall(_.leader == LeaderAndIsr.NoLeader)\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Completion criteria:**\n- All partitions have elected leaders\n- OR timeout expires\n- OR error occurs\n\n---\n\n### 7. CONTROLLER - PARTITION LEADER ELECTION\n\nWhen ZooKeeper watches trigger (via `/brokers/topics/{topic}` change):\n\n1. **KafkaController** detects new topic partitions\n2. Assigns partition leaders from replica list (typically first replica)\n3. Sends **LeaderAndIsrRequest** to brokers with new partition state\n4. Brokers acknowledge with partition logs\n\n---\n\n### 8. PARTITION AND LOG CREATION ON BROKERS\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/log/LogManager.scala`\n\nWhen `LeaderAndIsrRequest` arrives, brokers create the actual log storage via `getOrCreateLog`:\n\n```scala\ndef getOrCreateLog(topicPartition: TopicPartition, \n                   isNew: Boolean = false, \n                   isFuture: Boolean = false,\n                   topicId: Option[Uuid], \n                   targetLogDirectoryId: Option[Uuid] = Option.empty): UnifiedLog = {\n    \n    logCreationOrDeletionLock synchronized {\n        val log = getLog(topicPartition, isFuture).getOrElse {\n            if (!isNew && offlineLogDirs.nonEmpty)\n                throw new KafkaStorageException(...)\n            \n            // Determine log directory\n            val logDirs: List[File] = {\n                // Prefer specified directory or use next available\n                if (preferredLogDir != null)\n                    List(new File(preferredLogDir))\n                else\n                    nextLogDirs()  // Round-robin across data directories\n            }\n            \n            val logDirName = if (isFuture)\n                UnifiedLog.logFutureDirName(topicPartition)\n            else\n                UnifiedLog.logDirName(topicPartition)\n            \n            // Create: /log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/\n            val logDir = logDirs.map(createLogDirectory(_, logDirName))\n                .find(_.isSuccess).get\n            \n            // Create UnifiedLog instance\n            val config = fetchLogConfig(topicPartition.topic)\n            val log = UnifiedLog(\n                dir = logDir,\n                config = config,\n                logStartOffset = 0L,\n                recoveryPoint = 0L,\n                maxTransactionTimeoutMs = maxTransactionTimeoutMs,\n                producerStateManagerConfig = producerStateManagerConfig,\n                scheduler = scheduler,\n                time = time,\n                brokerTopicStats = brokerTopicStats,\n                logDirFailureChannel = logDirFailureChannel,\n                topicId = topicId,\n                keepPartitionMetadataFile = keepPartitionMetadataFile,\n                remoteStorageSystemEnable = remoteStorageSystemEnable)\n            \n            currentLogs.put(topicPartition, log)\n            info(s\"Created log for partition $topicPartition in $logDir\")\n            log\n        }\n        \n        // Assign topic ID if needed\n        if (log.topicId.isEmpty) {\n            topicId.foreach(log.assignTopicId)\n        }\n        \n        log\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Files created on disk:**\n- `/log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/` directory\n- Index files (`.index`, `.timeindex`)\n- Log segment files (`.log`)\n- Leader epoch cache (`.leader-epoch-checkpoint`)\n\n---\n\n### 9. REPLICA MANAGER - LEADERSHIP ESTABLISHMENT\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala`\n\n```scala\ndef becomeLeaderOrFollower(correlationId: Int,\n                           leaderAndIsrRequest: LeaderAndIsrRequest,\n                           onLeadershipChange: (...) => Unit): LeaderAndIsrResponse = {\n    // Process partition state changes\n    val partitionStates = request.partitionStates() // Map of TopicPartition -> LeaderAndIsrPartitionState\n    \n    val (leaderPartitions, followerPartitions) = \n        partitionStates.partition { case (_, state) =>\n            state.leader == brokerId\n        }\n    \n    // Make this broker leader for assigned partitions\n    val makeLeadersResult = makeLeaders(controllerId, controllerEpoch, \n        leaderPartitions, correlationId)\n    \n    // Make this broker follower for other partitions  \n    val makeFollowersResult = makeFollowers(controllerId, controllerEpoch,\n        followerPartitions, leaderPartitions, correlationId)\n    \n    // Returns response with partition errors\n}\n```\n\nFor each new partition, the ReplicaManager calls LogManager.getOrCreateLog() to create the storage.\n\n---\n\n### 10. METADATA UPDATES - ZOOKEEPER TO BROKERS\n\n**Metadata cache update flow:**\n\n1. **ZooKeeper watcher** detects `/brokers/topics/{topic}` node creation\n2. **KafkaController** reads assignment from ZK\n3. **Controller sends LeaderAndIsrRequest** to all replicas with leader assignment\n4. **Brokers update MetadataCache** with new partition metadata\n5. **Brokers create partition logs** via LogManager\n6. **Brokers reply to LeaderAndIsrRequest**\n7. **Controller updates ISR** (in-sync replicas) in ZK after replicas ACK\n\n---\n\n### 11. COMPLETE TOPIC CREATION SEQUENCE DIAGRAM\n\n```\nClient (TopicCommand)\n  |\n  +---> Admin API\n         |\n         +---> CreateTopicsRequest\n                |\n                v\n         Broker (KafkaApis)\n          |\n          +---> Check if controller active\n          |\n          +---> Authorize request\n          |\n          +---> ZkAdminManager.createTopics()\n                |\n                +---> Validate topic doesn't exist\n                |\n                +---> Calculate replica assignments\n                |\n                +---> Write to ZooKeeper:\n                |     - ADMIN MANAGER - TOPIC CREATION ORCHESTRATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ZkAdminManager.scala`\n\nThe `createTopics` method (lines 159-258) performs the core topic creation logic:\n\n```scala\ndef createTopics(timeout: Int,\n                 validateOnly: Boolean,\n                 toCreate: Map[String, CreatableTopic],\n                 includeConfigsAndMetadata: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n                 controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n                 responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit): Unit = {\n\n    // 1. Validate and prepare metadata for each topic\n    val brokers = metadataCache.getAliveBrokers()\n    val metadata = toCreate.values.map(topic => {\n        // Check topic doesn't already exist\n        if (metadataCache.contains(topic.name))\n            throw new TopicExistsException(...)\n        \n        // Resolve replica assignments (auto or manual)\n        val assignments = if (topic.assignments.isEmpty) {\n            // Auto-assign replicas to brokers\n            AdminUtils.assignReplicasToBrokers(\n                brokers, resolvedNumPartitions, resolvedReplicationFactor)\n        } else {\n            // Use provided manual assignments\n            topic.assignments.forEach { assignment =>\n                assignments(assignment.partitionIndex) = \n                    assignment.brokerIds.asScala.map(a => a: Int)\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Validate assignment and configuration\n        adminZkClient.validateTopicCreate(topic.name, assignments, configs)\n        validateTopicCreatePolicy(...)\n        \n        // Populate metadata and configs for response\n        maybePopulateMetadataAndConfigs(..., topic.name, configs, assignments)\n        \n        if (validateOnly) {\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        } else {\n            // Write to ZooKeeper\n            controllerMutationQuota.record(assignments.size)\n            adminZkClient.createTopicWithAssignment(\n                topic.name, configs, assignments, validate = false)\n            populateIds(...)\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        }\n    }).toBuffer\n    \n    // 2. Handle immediate responses or delayed operations\n    if (timeout <= 0 || validateOnly || !metadata.exists(...)) {\n        // Immediate response\n        responseCallback(results)\n    } else {\n        // 3. Create delayed operation and watch for partition leaders\n        val delayedCreate = new DelayedCreatePartitions(\n            timeout, metadata, this, responseCallback)\n        val delayedCreateKeys = toCreate.values.map(topic => TopicKey(topic.name))\n        topicPurgatory.tryCompleteElseWatch(delayedCreate, delayedCreateKeys)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key responsibilities:**\n- Validate topic doesn't exist\n- Calculate replica assignments (or use provided ones)\n- Write topic configs to ZooKeeper\n- Create partition assignments in ZooKeeper\n- Queue delayed operation to wait for leader election\n\n---\n\n### 5. ZOOKEEPER WRITES\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/zk/AdminZkClient.scala`\n\n```scala\ndef createTopicWithAssignment(topic: String,\n                              config: Properties,\n                              partitionReplicaAssignment: Map[Int, Seq[Int]],\n                              validate: Boolean = true,\n                              usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    \n    if (validate)\n        validateTopicCreate(topic, partitionReplicaAssignment, config)\n    \n    // Write topic configuration\n    zkClient.setOrCreateEntityConfigs(ConfigType.TOPIC, topic, config)\n    \n    // Create partition assignment in ZooKeeper\n    writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic, \n        partitionReplicaAssignment.map { case (k, v) => \n            k -> ReplicaAssignment(v) \n        },\n        isUpdate = false, \n        usesTopicId)\n}\n\nprivate def writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic: String, \n                                          replicaAssignment: Map[Int, ReplicaAssignment],\n                                          isUpdate: Boolean, \n                                          usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    val assignment = replicaAssignment.map { \n        case (partitionId, replicas) => \n            (new TopicPartition(topic, partitionId), replicas) \n    }.toMap\n    \n    if (!isUpdate) {\n        val topicIdOpt = if (usesTopicId) Some(Uuid.randomUuid()) else None\n        zkClient.createTopicAssignment(topic, topicIdOpt, \n            assignment.map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**ZooKeeper paths created:**\n- `/config/topics/{topic-name}` - topic configuration\n- `/brokers/topics/{topic-name}` - partition assignment\n- Topic ID is stored as part of the assignment\n\n---\n\n### 6. DELAYED OPERATION - WAITING FOR LEADERS\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DelayedCreatePartitions.scala`\n\nOnce the metadata is written to ZooKeeper, a `DelayedCreatePartitions` operation waits for all partition leaders to be elected:\n\n```scala\nclass DelayedCreatePartitions(delayMs: Long,\n                              createMetadata: Seq[CreatePartitionsMetadata],\n                              adminManager: ZkAdminManager,\n                              responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit)\n  extends DelayedOperation(delayMs) {\n  \n  override def tryComplete(): Boolean = {\n    val leaderlessPartitionCount = createMetadata\n        .filter(_.error.isSuccess)\n        .foldLeft(0) { case (topicCounter, metadata) =>\n            topicCounter + missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions)\n        }\n    \n    if (leaderlessPartitionCount == 0) {\n        trace(\"All partitions have a leader, completing the delayed operation\")\n        forceComplete()\n    } else {\n        false\n    }\n  }\n  \n  override def onComplete(): Unit = {\n    val results = createMetadata.map { metadata =>\n        if (metadata.error.isSuccess && \n            missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions) > 0)\n            (metadata.topic, new ApiError(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, null))\n        else\n            (metadata.topic, metadata.error)\n    }.toMap\n    responseCallback(results)\n  }\n  \n  private def isMissingLeader(topic: String, partition: Int): Boolean = {\n    val partitionInfo = adminManager.metadataCache.getPartitionInfo(topic, partition)\n    partitionInfo.forall(_.leader == LeaderAndIsr.NoLeader)\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Completion criteria:**\n- All partitions have elected leaders\n- OR timeout expires\n- OR error occurs\n\n---\n\n### 7. Assigns partition leaders from replica list (typically first replica)\n3. Sends **LeaderAndIsrRequest** to brokers with new partition state\n4. `getOrCreateLog`:\n\n```scala\ndef getOrCreateLog(topicPartition: TopicPartition, \n                   isNew: Boolean = false, \n                   isFuture: Boolean = false,\n                   topicId: Option[Uuid], \n                   targetLogDirectoryId: Option[Uuid] = Option.empty): UnifiedLog = {\n    \n    logCreationOrDeletionLock synchronized {\n        val log = getLog(topicPartition, isFuture).getOrElse {\n            if (!isNew && offlineLogDirs.nonEmpty)\n                throw new KafkaStorageException(...)\n            \n            // Determine log directory\n            val logDirs: List[File] = {\n                // Prefer specified directory or use next available\n                if (preferredLogDir != null)\n                    List(new File(preferredLogDir))\n                else\n                    nextLogDirs()  // Round-robin across data directories\n            }\n            \n            val logDirName = if (isFuture)\n                UnifiedLog.logFutureDirName(topicPartition)\n            else\n                UnifiedLog.logDirName(topicPartition)\n            \n            // Create: /log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/\n            val logDir = logDirs.map(createLogDirectory(_, logDirName))\n                .find(_.isSuccess).get\n            \n            // Create UnifiedLog instance\n            val config = fetchLogConfig(topicPartition.topic)\n            val log = UnifiedLog(\n                dir = logDir,\n                config = config,\n                logStartOffset = 0L,\n                recoveryPoint = 0L,\n                maxTransactionTimeoutMs = maxTransactionTimeoutMs,\n                producerStateManagerConfig = producerStateManagerConfig,\n                scheduler = scheduler,\n                time = time,\n                brokerTopicStats = brokerTopicStats,\n                logDirFailureChannel = logDirFailureChannel,\n                topicId = topicId,\n                keepPartitionMetadataFile = keepPartitionMetadataFile,\n                remoteStorageSystemEnable = remoteStorageSystemEnable)\n            \n            currentLogs.put(topicPartition, log)\n            info(s\"Created log for partition $topicPartition in $logDir\")\n            log\n        }\n        \n        // Assign topic ID if needed\n        if (log.topicId.isEmpty) {\n            topicId.foreach(log.assignTopicId)\n        }\n        \n        log\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Files created on disk:**\n- `/log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/` directory\n- Index files (`.index`, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015iNXoo1HgCSHpWjjqYdnKS","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→Trogdor\n     2→========================================\n     3→Trogdor is a test framework for Apache Kafka.\n     4→\n     5→Trogdor can run benchmarks and other workloads.  Trogdor can also inject faults in order to stress test the system.\n     6→\n     7→Quickstart\n     8→=========================================================\n     9→First, we want to start a single-node Kafka cluster with a ZooKeeper and a broker.\n    10→\n    11→Running ZooKeeper:\n    12→\n    13→```\n    14→ ./bin/zookeeper-server-start.sh ./config/zookeeper.properties &> /tmp/zookeeper.log &\n    15→```\n    16→Running Kafka:\n    17→\n    18→```\n    19→./bin/kafka-server-start.sh ./config/server.properties &> /tmp/kafka.log &\n    20→```\n    21→Then, we want to run a Trogdor Agent, plus a Trogdor Coordinator.\n    22→\n    23→To run the Trogdor Agent:\n    24→\n    25→```\n    26→./bin/trogdor.sh agent -c ./config/trogdor.conf -n node0 &> /tmp/trogdor-agent.log &\n    27→```\n    28→To run the Trogdor Coordinator:\n    29→\n    30→```\n    31→./bin/trogdor.sh coordinator -c ./config/trogdor.conf -n node0 &> /tmp/trogdor-coordinator.log &\n    32→```\n    33→Let's confirm that all of the daemons are running:\n    34→\n    35→```\n    36→jps\n    37→116212 Coordinator\n    38→115188 QuorumPeerMain\n    39→116571 Jps\n    40→115420 Kafka\n    41→115694 Agent\n    42→```\n    43→Now, we can submit a test job to Trogdor.\n    44→\n    45→```\n    46→./bin/trogdor.sh client createTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0 --spec ./tests/spec/simple_produce_bench.json\n    47→Sent CreateTaskRequest for task produce0.\n    48→```\n    49→We can run showTask to see what the task's status is:\n    50→\n    51→```\n    52→./bin/trogdor.sh client showTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0\n    53→Task bar of type org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec is DONE. FINISHED at 2019-01-09T20:38:22.039-08:00 after 6s\n    54→```\n    55→To see the results, we use showTask with --show-status:\n    56→\n    57→```\n    58→./bin/trogdor.sh client showTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0 --show-status\n    59→Task bar of type org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec is DONE. FINISHED at 2019-01-09T20:38:22.039-08:00 after 6s\n    60→Status: {\n    61→  \"totalSent\" : 50000,\n    62→  \"averageLatencyMs\" : 17.83388,\n    63→  \"p50LatencyMs\" : 12,\n    64→  \"p95LatencyMs\" : 75,\n    65→  \"p99LatencyMs\" : 96,\n    66→  \"transactionsCommitted\" : 0\n    67→}\n    68→```\n    69→Trogdor Architecture\n    70→========================================\n    71→Trogdor has a single coordinator process which manages multiple agent processes.  Each agent process is responsible for a single cluster node.\n    72→\n    73→The Trogdor coordinator manages tasks.  A task is anything we might want to do on a cluster, such as running a benchmark, injecting a fault, or running a workload.  In order to implement each task, the coordinator creates workers on one or more agent nodes.\n    74→\n    75→The Trogdor agent process implements the tasks.  For example, when running a workload, the agent process is the process which produces and consumes messages.\n    76→\n    77→Both the coordinator and the agent expose a REST interface that accepts objects serialized via JSON.  There is also a command-line program which makes it easy to send messages to either one without manually crafting the JSON message body.\n    78→\n    79→All Trogdor RPCs are idempotent except the shutdown requests.  Sending an idempotent RPC twice in a row has the same effect as sending the RPC once.\n    80→\n    81→Tasks\n    82→========================================\n    83→Tasks are described by specifications containing:\n    84→\n    85→* A \"class\" field describing the task type.  This contains a full Java class name.\n    86→* A \"startMs\" field describing when the task should start.  This is given in terms of milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.\n    87→* A \"durationMs\" field describing how long the task should last.  This is given in terms of milliseconds.\n    88→* Other fields which are task-specific.\n    89→\n    90→The task specification is usually written as JSON.  For example, this task specification describes a network partition between nodes 1 and 2, and 3:\n    91→\n    92→    {\n    93→        \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.fault.NetworkPartitionFaultSpec\",\n    94→        \"startMs\": 1000,\n    95→        \"durationMs\": 30000,\n    96→        \"partitions\": [[\"node1\", \"node2\"], [\"node3\"]]\n    97→    }\n    98→    \n    99→This task runs a simple ProduceBench test on a cluster with one producer node, 5 topics, and 10,000 messages per second. \n   100→The keys are generated sequentially and the configured partitioner (DefaultPartitioner) is used. \n   101→\n   102→    {\n   103→        \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec\",\n   104→        \"durationMs\": 10000000,\n   105→        \"producerNode\": \"node0\",\n   106→        \"bootstrapServers\": \"localhost:9092\",\n   107→        \"targetMessagesPerSec\": 10000,\n   108→        \"maxMessages\": 50000,\n   109→        \"activeTopics\": {\n   110→            \"foo[1-3]\": {\n   111→                \"numPartitions\": 10,\n   112→                \"replicationFactor\": 1\n   113→            }\n   114→        },\n   115→        \"inactiveTopics\": {\n   116→             \"foo[4-5]\": {\n   117→                 \"numPartitions\": 10,\n   118→                 \"replicationFactor\": 1\n   119→             }\n   120→        },\n   121→        \"keyGenerator\": {\n   122→             \"type\": \"sequential\",\n   123→             \"size\": 8,\n   124→             \"offset\": 1\n   125→        },\n   126→        \"useConfiguredPartitioner\": true\n   127→     }\n   128→\n   129→Tasks are submitted to the coordinator.  Once the coordinator determines that it is time for the task to start, it creates workers on agent processes.  The workers run until the task is done.\n   130→\n   131→Task specifications are immutable; they do not change after the task has been created.\n   132→\n   133→Tasks can be in several states:\n   134→* PENDING, when task is waiting to execute,\n   135→* RUNNING, when the task is running,\n   136→* STOPPING, when the task is in the process of stopping,\n   137→* DONE, when the task is done.\n   138→\n   139→Tasks that are DONE also have an error field which will be set if the task failed.\n   140→\n   141→Workloads\n   142→========================================\n   143→Trogdor can run several workloads.  Workloads perform operations on the cluster and measure their performance.  Workloads fail when the operations cannot be performed.\n   144→\n   145→### ProduceBench\n   146→ProduceBench starts a Kafka producer on a single agent node, producing to several partitions.  The workload measures the average produce latency, as well as the median, 95th percentile, and 99th percentile latency.\n   147→It can be configured to use a transactional producer which can commit transactions based on a set time interval or number of messages.\n   148→\n   149→### RoundTripWorkload\n   150→RoundTripWorkload tests both production and consumption.  The workload starts a Kafka producer and consumer on a single node.  The consumer will read back the messages that were produced by the producer.\n   151→\n   152→### ConsumeBench\n   153→ConsumeBench starts one or more Kafka consumers on a single agent node. Depending on the passed in configuration (see ConsumeBenchSpec), the consumers either subscribe to a set of topics (leveraging consumer group functionality and dynamic partition assignment) or manually assign partitions to themselves.\n   154→The workload measures the average consume latency, as well as the median, 95th percentile, and 99th percentile latency.\n   155→\n   156→Faults\n   157→========================================\n   158→Trogdor can run several faults which deliberately break something in the cluster.\n   159→\n   160→### ProcessStopFault\n   161→ProcessStopFault stops a process by sending it a SIGSTOP signal.  When the fault ends, the process is resumed with SIGCONT.\n   162→\n   163→### NetworkPartitionFault\n   164→NetworkPartitionFault sets up an artificial network partition between one or more sets of nodes.  Currently, this is implemented using iptables.  The iptables rules are set up on the outbound traffic from the affected nodes.  Therefore, the affected nodes should still be reachable from outside the cluster.\n   165→\n   166→External Processes\n   167→========================================\n   168→Trogdor supports running arbitrary commands in external processes. This is a generic way to run any configurable command in the Trogdor framework - be it a Python program, bash script, docker image, etc.\n   169→\n   170→### ExternalCommandWorker\n   171→ExternalCommandWorker starts an external command defined by the ExternalCommandSpec. It essentially allows you to run any command on any Trogdor agent node.\n   172→The worker communicates with the external process via its stdin, stdout and stderr in a JSON protocol. It uses stdout for any actionable communication and only logs what it sees in stderr.\n   173→On startup the worker will first send a message describing the workload to the external process in this format:\n   174→```\n   175→{\"id\":<task ID string>, \"workload\":<configured workload JSON object>}\n   176→```\n   177→and will then listen for messages from the external process, again in a JSON format.\n   178→Said JSON can contain the following fields:\n   179→- status: If the object contains this field, the status of the worker will be set to the given value.\n   180→- error: If the object contains this field, the error of the worker will be set to the given value. Once an error occurs, the external process will be terminated.\n   181→- log: If the object contains this field, a log message will be issued with this text.\n   182→An example:\n   183→```json\n   184→{\"log\": \"Finished successfully.\", \"status\": {\"p99ProduceLatency\": \"100ms\", \"messagesSent\": 10000}}\n   185→```\n   186→\n   187→Exec Mode\n   188→========================================\n   189→Sometimes, you just want to run a test quickly on a single node.  In this case, you can use \"exec mode.\"  This mode allows you to run a single Trogdor Agent without a Coordinator.\n   190→\n   191→When using exec mode, you must pass in a Task specification to use.  The Agent will try to start this task.\n   192→\n   193→For example:\n   194→\n   195→```\n   196→./bin/trogdor.sh agent -n node0 -c ./config/trogdor.conf --exec ./tests/spec/simple_produce_bench.json\n   197→```\n   198→When using exec mode, the Agent will exit once the task is complete.\n   199→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_011ZXs8AhX2yBNvrqkW6uzav","session_id":"16d882b6-d079-42aa-a2b3-4e3b12e96f01","uuid":"99d8b3c3-4e93-471c-bc5d-ddf843da3467"}

Here's a comprehensive breakdown of testing frameworks, patterns, and infrastructure:\n\n---\n\n### 1. UNIT TESTING FRAMEWORK\n\n**Primary Framework: JUnit 5 (Jupiter)**\n- **Version**: 5.10.2\n- **Key annotations used**:\n  - `@Test` - marks individual test methods\n  - `@BeforeEach` - setup before each test\n  - `@AfterEach` - teardown after each test\n  - `@ParameterizedTest` - parameterized tests\n  - `@ValueSource` - simple parameter values\n  - `@MethodSource` - complex parameter sources\n  - `@Timeout` - test timeouts with thread mode control\n\n**Test Location Statistics**:\n- Java Unit Tests: 135 test files in `/workspace/clients/src/test/java`\n- Scala Unit Tests: 328 test files in `/workspace/core/src/test/scala`\n\n---\n\n### 2. TEST DEPENDENCIES\n\nFrom `/workspace/gradle/dependencies.gradle`:\n\n**Core Testing Libraries**:\n```gradle\n- junitJupiter: \"org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter:5.10.2\"\n- junitJupiterApi: \"org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter-api:5.10.2\"\n- junitPlatformSuiteEngine: \"org.junit.platform:junit-platform-suite-engine:1.10.2\"\n- junitPlatformLanucher: \"org.junit.platform:junit-platform-launcher:1.10.2\"\n```\n\n**Mocking Framework**:\n```gradle\n- Mockito: varies by Java/Scala version\n  - Java 11+: mockito-core 5.10.0 (inline strategy)\n  - Java 8 + Scala 2.13: mockito-inline 4.11.0\n  - Scala 2.12: mockito-inline 4.9.0\n- mockitoJunitJupiter: \"org.mockito:mockito-junit-jupiter:$mockitoVersion\"\n```\n\n**Assertion Libraries**:\n```gradle\n- hamcrest: \"2.2\"\n```\n\n**Property-Based Testing**:\n```gradle\n- jqwik: \"1.8.3\" - property-based testing framework\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. UNIT TEST EXAMPLES\n\n**Java Unit Test Structure** (`NetworkClientTest.java`):\n```java\npublic class NetworkClientTest {\n    protected final int defaultRequestTimeoutMs = 1000;\n    protected final MockTime time = new MockTime();\n    protected final MockSelector selector = new MockSelector(time);\n    protected final Node node = TestUtils.singletonCluster().nodes().iterator().next();\n    \n    // Uses static imports for assertions\n    // import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;\n    // import static org.mockito.Mockito.*;\n}\n```\n\n**Scala Unit Test Structure** (`KafkaRequestHandlerTest.scala`):\n```scala\nclass KafkaRequestHandlerTest {\n  val brokerTopicStats = new BrokerTopicStats(true)\n  val time = new MockTime()\n  \n  @Test\n  def testCallbackTiming(): Unit = {\n    // test implementation\n  }\n  \n  @Test\n  def testCallbackTryCompleteActions(): Unit = {\n    // test implementation\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Parameterized Test Example** (`PlaintextProducerSendTest.scala`):\n```scala\n@ParameterizedTest\n@ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\ndef testWrongSerializer(quorum: String): Unit = {\n  // runs once for each value in strings\n}\n\n@ParameterizedTest\n@MethodSource\ndef testMethod(args: Arguments): Unit = {\n  // uses method source for complex parameters\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. INTEGRATION TESTING\n\n**Test Harness Hierarchy**:\n\n```\nQuorumTestHarness (base - handles ZK or KRaft setup)\n    └── KafkaServerTestHarness (manages broker lifecycle)\n            └── IntegrationTestHarness (producer/consumer clients)\n                    └── BaseProducerSendTest / BaseConsumerTest / BaseAdminIntegrationTest\n```\n\n**Base Classes**:\n\n**QuorumTestHarness** (`/workspace/core/src/test/scala/unit/kafka/integration/KafkaServerTestHarness.scala`):\n- Sets up ZooKeeper or KRaft clusters\n- Implements `BeforeEach` and `AfterEach` lifecycle\n- Provides methods: `isZKTest()`, `isKRaftTest()`, `isBrokerTest()`\n- Trait implementations: `ZooKeeperQuorumImplementation`, `KRaftQuorumImplementation`\n\n**KafkaServerTestHarness**:\n- Manages broker lifecycle: `createBrokers()`, `shutdownServers()`\n- Configuration generation: `generateConfigs(): Seq[KafkaConfig]`\n- Helper methods: `bootstrapServers()`, `brokers: Buffer[KafkaBroker]`\n- Security configuration hooks: `configureSecurityBeforeServersStart()`, `configureSecurityAfterServersStart()`\n\n**IntegrationTestHarness** (extends `KafkaServerTestHarness`):\n- Manages producer/consumer/admin clients\n- Configuration properties: `producerConfig`, `consumerConfig`, `adminClientConfig`\n- Lifecycle: maintains buffers of clients for cleanup\n- Helper: `modifyConfigs(props: Seq[Properties]): Unit`\n- Methods: `doSetup()`, `doSuperuserSetup()` for security tests\n\n**Example Integration Test** (`PlaintextProducerSendTest.scala`):\n```scala\nclass PlaintextProducerSendTest extends BaseProducerSendTest {\n  @ParameterizedTest\n  @ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\n  def testWrongSerializer(quorum: String): Unit = {\n    val producerProps = new Properties()\n    producerProps.put(ProducerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n    val producer = registerProducer(new KafkaProducer(producerProps))\n    assertThrows(classOf[SerializationException], () => producer.send(record))\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. TEST UTILITIES AND HELPERS\n\n**Test Utility Classes**:\n\n**`TestUtils`** (`/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/test/TestUtils.java`):\n- File operations: `tempDir()`, `tempFile()`\n- Random data generation: `SEEDED_RANDOM`, `RANDOM`\n- Constants: `LETTERS`, `DIGITS`, `LETTERS_AND_DIGITS`\n- Cluster helpers: `singletonCluster()`, `createCluster()`\n- Client creation: `createAdminClient()`, `createConsumer()`, `createProducer()`\n- Polling utilities: `waitForCondition()` with retries\n- Message helpers: `RequestTestUtils`, `ByteBufferChannel`\n\n**Mock Objects**:\n- `MockTime` - controllable time for testing\n- `MockSelector` - network selector simulation\n- `MockClient` - HTTP client mock for admin operations\n- `MockMetadataUpdater` - metadata management mock\n- `MockMetricsReporter`, `MockSerializer`, `MockDeserializer`\n- `MockPartitioner`, `MockConsumerInterceptor`, `MockProducerInterceptor`\n\n**Admin Client Testing**:\n\n`AdminClientUnitTestEnv.java`:\n```java\npublic class AdminClientUnitTestEnv implements AutoCloseable {\n    private final Time time;\n    private final Cluster cluster;\n    private final MockClient mockClient;\n    private final KafkaAdminClient adminClient;\n    \n    // Constructor setups mock infrastructure\n    // Use try-with-resources for automatic cleanup\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. TEST PATTERNS AND CONVENTIONS\n\n**Naming Conventions**:\n- Unit Tests: `*Test.scala` or `*Test.java`\n- Integration Tests: `*IntegrationTest.scala` or `*IntegrationTest.java`\n- Parameterized Tests: Same naming, use `@ParameterizedTest` annotation\n- Test methods: `test<Feature>()` pattern\n\n**Common Test Patterns**:\n\n1. **Setup/Teardown with JUnit 5**:\n   ```scala\n   @BeforeEach\n   override def setUp(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {\n     super.setUp(testInfo)\n     // initialization\n   }\n   \n   @AfterEach\n   override def tearDown(): Unit = {\n     TestUtils.shutdownServers(_brokers)\n     super.tearDown()\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Try-with-resources for Auto-cleanup**:\n   ```java\n   try (AdminClientUnitTestEnv env = new AdminClientUnitTestEnv(cluster)) {\n       Admin admin = env.adminClient();\n       // test code\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Mocking with Mockito**:\n   ```scala\n   val apiHandler = mock(classOf[ApiRequestHandler])\n   when(apiHandler.handle(any(), any())).thenAnswer { _ =>\n     // behavior\n   }\n   verify(apiHandler, times(2)).handle(any(), any())\n   ```\n\n4. **Wait for Condition Pattern**:\n   ```java\n   TestUtils.waitForCondition(() -> condition.isMet(), \n       DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_MS, \"Condition not met\");\n   ```\n\n5. **Timeout Control**:\n   ```scala\n   @Timeout(value = 15, unit = TimeUnit.SECONDS, \n            threadMode = Timeout.ThreadMode.SEPARATE_THREAD)\n   def testLongRunning(): Unit = { }\n   ```\n\n---\n\n### 7. INTEGRATION TESTING WITH CLUSTERS\n\n**Security Protocol Support**:\n- PLAINTEXT\n- SSL (with TrustStore/KeyStore)\n- SASL (PLAIN, SCRAM, OAUTHBEARER, GSSAPI)\n\n**Configuration Builders** (in `IntegrationTestHarness`):\n```scala\nprotected def securityProtocol: SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT\nprotected def listenerName: ListenerName = ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol)\nprotected def trustStoreFile: Option[File] = None\nprotected def serverSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\nprotected def clientSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\n```\n\n**Cluster Types Supported**:\n- **ZooKeeper-based**: Traditional ZK-managed clusters\n- **KRaft-based**: Kraft metadata mode (KIP-500)\n- **Hybrid**: ZK migration mode\n\n---\n\n### 8. TROGDOR - PERFORMANCE & WORKLOAD TESTING\n\n**Location**: `/workspace/trogdor`\n\n**Purpose**: Distributed performance testing and fault injection framework\n\n**Architecture**:\n- **Coordinator**: Central task manager\n- **Agents**: Run on cluster nodes, execute tasks\n- **Tasks**: Specifications in JSON format\n\n**Task Types**:\n\n1. **Workloads** (performance testing):\n   - `ProduceBenchSpec`: Measures producer latency (p50, p95, p99)\n   - `ConsumeBenchSpec`: Measures consumer latency\n   - `RoundTripWorkload`: End-to-end latency testing\n   - `ConnectionStressSpec`: Connection pool testing\n   - `SustainedConnectionSpec`: Long-duration connection testing\n\n2. **Faults** (chaos engineering):\n   - `ProcessStopFault`: SIGSTOP/SIGCONT signals\n   - `NetworkPartitionFault`: iptables-based network isolation\n   - `DegradedNetworkFault`: Latency/packet loss injection\n   - `KiboshFault`: Disk I/O manipulation\n   - `FilesUnreadableFault`: Permission changes\n\n3. **Task States**: PENDING → RUNNING → STOPPING → DONE\n\n**Task Specification Format**:\n```json\n{\n  \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec\",\n  \"startMs\": 1000,\n  \"durationMs\": 30000,\n  \"producerNode\": \"node0\",\n  \"bootstrapServers\": \"localhost:9092\",\n  \"targetMessagesPerSec\": 10000,\n  \"activeTopics\": {\n    \"foo[1-3]\": {\n      \"numPartitions\": 10,\n      \"replicationFactor\": 1\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Features**:\n- Histogram tracking for latency percentiles\n- Payload generators (constant, Gaussian, random, timestamp-based)\n- Throughput generators with configurable patterns\n- JSON REST API for task management\n- Idempotent RPC operations\n\n---\n\n### 9. TEST CONFIGURATION & ENVIRONMENT\n\n**JVM Settings** (from `build.gradle`):\n```gradle\ndefaultMaxHeapSize = \"2g\"\ndefaultJvmArgs = [\"-Xss4m\", \"-XX:+UseParallelGC\"]\n\n// Java 16+ special handling for module access\nif (JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_16))\n  defaultJvmArgs.addAll(\n    \"--add-opens=java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    \"--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    // ... more module access configurations\n  )\n```\n\n**Test Compiler Options**:\n- Parameter annotations enabled: `-parameters` flag\n- This enables `TestInfo#getDisplayName()` with actual parameter names\n\n**Test Properties**:\n- `maxParallelForks`: Parallelization control\n- `maxScalaacThreads`: Scala compiler threads\n- `ignoreFailures`: Soft failure mode\n- `maxTestRetries`, `maxTestRetryFailures`: Flaky test handling\n- `testLoggingEvents`: Custom logging configuration\n\n---\n\n### 10. SPECIAL TESTING TOOLS\n\n**TestUtils Features**:\n- Temporary file/directory management\n- Random string/byte array generation\n- Cluster metadata creation\n- Request serialization helpers\n- Exit code mocking\n- Network receive simulation\n\n**Advanced Mocking**:\n- `DelayedReceive`: Simulates network delays\n- `MockSelector`: Mocks network I/O\n- `MockPartitioner`: Custom partition assignment\n- `CounterConsumerRebalanceListener`: Rebalance tracking\n\n---\n\n### 11. 115→  private def typeOf(name: String): Option[ConfigDef.Type] = Option(configDef.configKeys.get(name)).map(_.`type`)\n   116→\n   117→  def configType(configName: String): Option[ConfigDef.Type] = getList(KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG).asScala.map {\n   332→      case \"broker\" => ProcessRole.BrokerRole\n   333→      case \"controller\" => ProcessRole.ControllerRole\n   334→      case role => throw new ConfigException(s\"Unknown process role '$role'\" +\n   335→        \" (only 'broker' and 'controller' are allowed roles)\")\n   336→    }\n   337→\n   338→    val distinctRoles: `${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}`: $roles\")\n   342→    }\n   343→\n   344→    distinctRoles\n   345→  }\n   346→\n   347→  def isKRaftCombinedMode: Boolean = {\n   348→    processRoles == val earlyStartListeners: Set[ListenerName] = {\n   397→    val listenersSet = listeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   398→    val controllerListenersSet = 1000L\n   697→    val millisInHour = 60L * millisInMinute\n   698→\n   699→    val millis: java.lang.Long =\n   700→      Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(\n   701→        Option(getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG)) match {\n   702→          case Some(mins) => millisInMinute * mins\n   703→          case None => getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG) * millisInHour\n   704→        })\n   705→\n   706→    if (millis < 0) return -1\n   707→    millis\n   708→  }\n   709→\n   710→  private def getMap(propName: String, propValue: String): Map[String, String] = {\n   711→    try {\n   712→      Csv.parseCsvMap(propValue).asScala\n   713→    } catch {\n   714→      case e: Exception => throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Error parsing configuration property '%s': %s\".format(propName, e.getMessage))\n   715→    }\n   716→  }\n   717→\n   718→  def listeners: Seq[EndPoint] =\n   719→    CoreUtils.listenerListToEndPoints(getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG), effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap)\n   720→\n   721→  def controllerListenerNames: Seq[String] = {\n   722→    val value = Option(getString(KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG)).getOrElse(\"\")\n   723→    if (value.isEmpty) {\n   724→      Seq.empty\n   725→    } else {\n   726→      value.split(\",\")\n   727→    }\n   728→  }\n   729→\n   730→  def controllerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] =\n   731→    listeners.filter(l => controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value()))\n   732→\n   733→  def saslMechanismControllerProtocol: String = getString(KRaftConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   734→\n   735→  def controlPlaneListener: Option[EndPoint] = {\n   736→    controlPlaneListenerName.map { listenerName =>\n   737→      listeners.filter(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.value() == listenerName.value()).head\n   738→    }\n   739→  }\n   740→\n   741→  def dataPlaneListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   742→    listeners.filterNot { listener =>\n   743→      val name = listener.listenerName.value()\n   744→      name.equals(getString(SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) ||\n   745→        controllerListenerNames.contains(name)\n   746→    }\n   747→  }\n   748→\n   749→  def effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   750→    val controllerAdvertisedListeners = advertisedListeners.filter(l => controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value()))\n   751→    val controllerListenersValue = controllerListeners\n   752→\n   753→    controllerListenerNames.flatMap { name =>\n   754→      controllerAdvertisedListeners\n   755→        .find(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.equals(ListenerName.normalised(name)))\n   756→        .orElse(controllerListenersValue.find(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.equals(ListenerName.normalised(name))))\n   757→    }\n   758→  }\n   759→\n   760→  def effectiveAdvertisedBrokerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   761→    advertisedListeners.filter(l => {\n   762→      if (!controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value())) {\n   763→        true\n   764→      } else if (migrationEnabled && Some(l.listenerName.value()).equals(controlPlaneListener.map(_.listenerName.value()))) {\n   765→        // KAFKA-17788: during ZK migration, always include control.plane.listener.name\n   766→        // in advertisedBrokerListeners.\n   767→        true\n   768→      } else {\n   769→        false\n   770→      }\n   771→    })\n   772→  }\n   773→\n   774→  // Use advertised listeners if defined, fallback to listeners otherwise\n   775→  private def advertisedListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   776→    val advertisedListenersProp = getString(SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG)\n   777→    if (advertisedListenersProp != null) {\n   778→      CoreUtils.listenerListToEndPoints(advertisedListenersProp, effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap, requireDistinctPorts=false)\n   779→    } else {\n   780→      listeners\n   781→    }\n   782→  }\n   783→\n   784→  private def getInterBrokerListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol: (ListenerName, SecurityProtocol) = {\n   785→    Option(getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) match {\n   786→      case Some(_) if originals.containsKey(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG) =>\n   787→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Only one of ${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} and \" +\n   788→          s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG} should be set.\")\n   789→      case Some(name) =>\n   790→        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   791→        val securityProtocol = effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.getOrElse(listenerName,\n   792→          throw new ConfigException(s\"Listener with name ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   793→            s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}.\"))\n   794→        (listenerName, securityProtocol)\n   795→      case None =>\n   796→        val securityProtocol = getSecurityProtocol(getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG),\n   797→          ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   798→        (ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol), securityProtocol)\n   799→    }\n   800→  }\n   801→\n   802→  private def getControlPlaneListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol: Option[(ListenerName, SecurityProtocol)] = {\n   803→    Option(getString(SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) match {\n   804→      case Some(name) =>\n   805→        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   806→        val securityProtocol = effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.getOrElse(listenerName,\n   807→          throw new ConfigException(s\"Listener with ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   808→            s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}.\"))\n   809→        Some(listenerName, securityProtocol)\n   810→\n   811→      case None => None\n   812→   }\n   813→  }\n   814→\n   815→  private def getSecurityProtocol(protocolName: String, configName: String): SecurityProtocol = {\n   816→    try SecurityProtocol.forName(protocolName)\n   817→    catch {\n   818→      case _: IllegalArgumentException =>\n   819→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Invalid security protocol `$protocolName` defined in $configName\")\n   820→    }\n   821→  }\n   822→\n   823→  def effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap: Map[ListenerName, SecurityProtocol] = {\n   824→    val mapValue = getMap(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG))\n   825→      .map { case (listenerName, protocolName) =>\n   826→        ListenerName.normalised(listenerName) -> getSecurityProtocol(protocolName, SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG)\n   827→      }\n   828→    if (usesSelfManagedQuorum && !originals.containsKey(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG)) {\n   829→      // Nothing was specified explicitly for listener.security.protocol.map, so we are using the default value,\n   830→      // and we are using KRaft.\n   831→      // Add PLAINTEXT mappings for controller listeners as long as there is no SSL or SASL_{PLAINTEXT,SSL} in use\n   832→      def isSslOrSasl(name: String): Boolean = name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SSL.name) || name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL.name) || name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT.name)\n   833→      // check controller listener names (they won't appear in listeners when process.roles=broker)\n   834→      // as well as listeners for occurrences of SSL or SASL_*\n   835→      if (controllerListenerNames.exists(isSslOrSasl) ||\n   836→        Csv.parseCsvList(getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG)).asScala.exists(listenerValue => isSslOrSasl(EndPoint.parseListenerName(listenerValue)))) {\n   837→        mapValue // don't add default mappings since we found something that is SSL or SASL_*\n   838→      } else {\n   839→        // add the PLAINTEXT mappings for all controller listener names that are not explicitly PLAINTEXT\n   840→        mapValue ++ controllerListenerNames.filterNot(SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT.name.equals(_)).map(\n   841→          new ListenerName(_) -> SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT)\n   842→      }\n   843→    } else {\n   844→      mapValue\n   845→    }\n   846→  }\n   847→\n   848→  // Topic IDs are used with all self-managed quorum clusters and ZK cluster with IBP greater than or equal to 2.8\n   849→  def usesTopicId: Boolean =\n   850→    usesSelfManagedQuorum || interBrokerProtocolVersion.isTopicIdsSupported\n   851→\n   852→  validateValues()\n   853→\n   854→  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   855→  private def validateValues(): Unit = {\n   856→    if (nodeId != brokerId) {\n   857→      throw new ConfigException(s\"You must set `${KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG}` to the same value as `${ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG}`.\")\n   858→    }\n   859→    if (requiresZookeeper) {\n   860→      if (zkConnect == null) {\n   861→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Missing required configuration `${ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECT_CONFIG}` which has no default value.\")\n   862→      }\n   863→      if (brokerIdGenerationEnable) {\n   864→        if (migrationEnabled) {\n   865→          require(brokerId >= 0, \"broker.id generation is incompatible with ZooKeeper migration. Please stop using it before enabling migration (set broker.id to a value greater or equal to 0).\")\n   866→        }\n   867→        require(brokerId >= -1 && brokerId <= maxReservedBrokerId, \"broker.id must be greater than or equal to -1 and not greater than reserved.broker.max.id\")\n   868→      } else {\n   869→        require(brokerId >= 0, \"broker.id must be greater than or equal to 0\")\n   870→      }\n   871→    } else {\n   872→      // KRaft-based metadata quorum\n   873→      if (nodeId < 0) {\n   874→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Missing configuration `${KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG}` which is required \" +\n   875→          s\"when `process.roles` is defined (i.e. when running in KRaft mode).\")\n   876→      }\n   877→      if (migrationEnabled) {\n   878→        if (zkConnect == null) {\n   879→          throw new ConfigException(s\"If using `${KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG}` in KRaft mode, `${ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECT_CONFIG}` must also be set.\")\n   880→        }\n   881→      }\n   882→    }\n   883→    require(logRollTimeMillis >= 1, \"log.roll.ms must be greater than or equal to 1\")\n   884→    require(logRollTimeJitterMillis >= 0, \"log.roll.jitter.ms must be greater than or equal to 0\")\n   885→    require(logRetentionTimeMillis >= 1 || logRetentionTimeMillis == -1, \"log.retention.ms must be unlimited (-1) or, greater than or equal to 1\")\n   886→    require(logDirs.nonEmpty, \"At least one log directory must be defined via log.dirs or log.dir.\")\n   887→    require(logCleanerDedupeBufferSize / logCleanerThreads > 1024 * 1024, \"log.cleaner.dedupe.buffer.size must be at least 1MB per cleaner thread.\")\n   888→    require(replicaFetchWaitMaxMs <= replicaSocketTimeoutMs, \"replica.socket.timeout.ms should always be at least replica.fetch.wait.max.ms\" +\n   889→      \" to prevent unnecessary socket timeouts\")\n   890→    require(replicaFetchWaitMaxMs <= replicaLagTimeMaxMs, \"replica.fetch.wait.max.ms should always be less than or equal to replica.lag.time.max.ms\" +\n   891→      \" to prevent frequent changes in ISR\")\n   892→\n   893→    val advertisedBrokerListenerNames = effectiveAdvertisedBrokerListeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   894→\n   895→    // validate KRaft-related configs\n   896→    val voterIds = QuorumConfig.parseVoterIds(quorumVoters)\n   897→    def validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft(): Unit = {\n   898→      if (voterIds.isEmpty && quorumBootstrapServers.isEmpty) {\n   899→        throw new ConfigException(\n   900→          s\"\"\"If using ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}, either ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG} must\n   901→          |contain the set of bootstrap controllers or ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG} must contain a parseable\n   902→          |set of controllers.\"\"\".stripMargin.replace(\"\\n\", \" \")\n   903→        )\n   904→      }\n   905→    }\n   906→    def validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForMigration(): Unit = {\n   907→      if (voterIds.isEmpty && quorumBootstrapServers.isEmpty) {\n   908→        throw new ConfigException(\n   909→          s\"\"\"If using ${KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG}, either ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG} must\n   910→          |contain the set of bootstrap controllers or ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG} must contain a parseable\n   911→          |set of controllers.\"\"\".stripMargin.replace(\"\\n\", \" \")\n   912→        )\n   913→      }\n   914→    }\n   915→    def validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft(): Unit = {\n   916→      require(controlPlaneListenerName.isEmpty,\n   917→        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} is not supported in KRaft mode.\")\n   918→    }\n   919→    def validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   920→      require(voterIds.isEmpty || voterIds.contains(nodeId),\n   921→        s\"If ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG} contains the 'controller' role, the node id $nodeId must be included in the set of voters ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG}=${voterIds.asScala.toSet}\")\n   922→    }\n   923→    def validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   924→      require(effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.nonEmpty,\n   925→        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must contain at least one value appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running the KRaft controller role\")\n   926→    }\n   927→    def validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   928→      val listenerNameValues = listeners.map(_.listenerName.value).toSet\n   929→      require(controllerListenerNames.forall(cln => listenerNameValues.contains(cln)),\n   930→        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must only contain values appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running the KRaft controller role\")\n   931→    }\n   932→    def validateAdvertisedBrokerListenersNonEmptyForBroker(): Unit = {\n   933→      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   934→        \"There must be at least one broker advertised listener.\" + (\n   935→          if (processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) s\" Perhaps all listeners appear in ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG}?\" else \"\"))\n   936→    }\n   937→    if (processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n   938→      // KRaft broker-only\n   939→      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   940→      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   941→      // nodeId must not appear in controller.quorum.voters\n   942→      require(!voterIds.contains(nodeId),\n   943→        s\"If ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG} contains just the 'broker' role, the node id $nodeId must not be included in the set of voters ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG}=${voterIds.asScala.toSet}\")\n   944→      // controller.listener.names must be non-empty...\n   945→      require(controllerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   946→        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must contain at least one value when running KRaft with just the broker role\")\n   947→      // controller.listener.names are forbidden in listeners...\n   948→      require(controllerListeners.isEmpty,\n   949→        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must not contain a value appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running KRaft with just the broker role\")\n   950→      // controller.listener.names must all appear in listener.security.protocol.map\n   951→      controllerListenerNames.foreach { name =>\n   952→        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   953→        if (!effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.contains(listenerName)) {\n   954→          throw new ConfigException(s\"Controller listener with name ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   955→            s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}  (an explicit security mapping for each controller listener is required if ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG} is non-empty, or if there are security protocols other than PLAINTEXT in use)\")\n   956→        }\n   957→      }\n   958→      // warn that only the first controller listener is used if there is more than one\n   959→      if (controllerListenerNames.size > 1) {\n   960→        warn(s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} has multiple entries; only the first will be used since ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}=broker: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n   961→      }\n   962→    } else if (processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.ControllerRole)) {\n   963→      // KRaft controller-only\n   964→      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   965→      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   966→      // listeners should only contain listeners also enumerated in the controller listener\n   967→      require(\n   968→        effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.size == listeners.size,\n   969→        s\"The ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG} config must only contain KRaft controller listeners from ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} when ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}=controller\"\n   970→      )\n   971→      validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController()\n   972→      validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController()\n   973→      validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController()\n   974→    } else if (isKRaftCombinedMode) {\n   975→      // KRaft combined broker and controller\n   976→      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   977→      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   978→      validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController()\n   979→      validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController()\n   980→      validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController()\n   981→    } else {\n   982→      // ZK-based\n   983→      if (migrationEnabled) {\n   984→        require(brokerId >= 0,\n   985→          \"broker.id generation is incompatible with ZooKeeper migration. Please stop using it before enabling migration (set broker.id to a value greater or equal to 0).\")\n   986→        validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForMigration()\n   987→        require(controllerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   988→          s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must not be empty when running in ZooKeeper migration mode: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n   989→        require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.isMigrationSupported, s\"Cannot enable ZooKeeper migration without setting \" +\n   990→          s\"'${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}' to 3.4 or higher\")\n   991→        if (logDirs.size > 1) {\n   992→          require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.isDirectoryAssignmentSupported,\n   993→            s\"Cannot enable ZooKeeper migration with multiple log directories (aka JBOD) without setting \" +\n   994→            s\"'${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}' to ${MetadataVersion.IBP_3_7_IV2} or higher\")\n   995→        }\n   996→      } else {\n   997→        // controller listener names must be empty when not in KRaft mode\n   998→        require(controllerListenerNames.isEmpty,\n   999→          s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must be empty when not running in KRaft mode: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n  1000→      }\n  1001→    }\n  1002→\n  1003→    val listenerNames = listeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n  1004→    if (processRoles.isEmpty || processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n  1005→      // validations for all broker setups (i.e. ZooKeeper and KRaft broker-only and KRaft co-located)\n  1006→      validateAdvertisedBrokerListenersNonEmptyForBroker()\n  1007→      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.contains(interBrokerListenerName),\n  1008→        s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} must be a listener name defined in ${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG}. \" +\n  1009→          s\"The valid options based on The valid values and visibility of a configuration\n    47→ * may change according to the values of other configurations. You can override {@link Recommender} to get valid\n    48→ * values and set visibility of a configuration given the current configuration values.\n    49→ *\n    50→ * <p/>\n    51→ * To use the class:\n    52→ * <p/>\n    53→ * <pre>\n    54→ * ConfigDef defs = new ConfigDef();\n    55→ *\n    56→ * // check {@link #define(String, Type, Object, Importance, String)} for more details.\n    57→ 79→ * functionality for accessing configs.\n    80→ */\n    81→public class ConfigDef {\n    82→\n    83→    private static final Pattern COMMA_WITH_WHITESPACE = Pattern.compile(\"\\\\s*,\\\\s*\");\n    84→\n    85→    /**\n    86→     * A unique Java object which represents the lack of a default value.\n    87→     */\n    88→    public static final Object NO_DEFAULT_VALUE = new Object();\n    89→\n    90→    private final Map<String, ConfigKey> configKeys;\n    91→    private final List<String> groups;\n    92→    private Set<String> configsWithNoParent;\n    93→\n    94→    public ConfigDef() {\n    95→        configKeys = new LinkedHashMap<>();\n    96→        groups = new LinkedList<>();\n    97→        configsWithNoParent = null;\n    98→    }\n    99→\n   100→    public ConfigDef(ConfigDef base) {\n   101→        configKeys = new LinkedHashMap<>(base.configKeys);\n   102→        groups = new LinkedList<>(base.groups);\n   103→        // It is not safe to copy this from the parent because we may subsequently add to the set of configs and\n   104→        // invalidate this\n   105→        configsWithNoParent = null;\n   106→    }\n   107→\n   108→    /**\n   109→     * Returns unmodifiable set of properties names defined in this {@linkplain ConfigDef}\n   110→     *\n   111→     * @return new unmodifiable {@link Set} instance containing the keys\n   112→     */\n   113→    public Set<String> names() {\n   114→        return Collections.unmodifiableSet(configKeys.keySet());\n   115→    }\n   116→\n   117→    public Map<String, Object> defaultValues() {\n   118→        Map<String, Object> defaultValues = new HashMap<>();\n   119→        for (ConfigKey key : configKeys.values()) {\n   120→            if (key.defaultValue != NO_DEFAULT_VALUE)\n   121→                defaultValues.put(key.name, key.defaultValue);\n   122→        }\n   123→        return defaultValues;\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    public ConfigDef define(ConfigKey key) {\n   127→        if (configKeys.containsKey(key.name)) {\n   128→            throw new ConfigException(\"Configuration \" + key.name + \" is defined twice.\");\n   129→        }\n   130→        if (key.group != null && !groups.contains(key.group)) {\n   131→            groups.add(key.group);\n   132→        }\n   133→        configKeys.put(key.name, key);\n   134→        return this;\n   135→    }\n   136→\n   137→    /**\n   138→     * Define a new configuration\n   139→     * @param name          the name of the config parameter\n   140→     * @param type          the type of the config\n   141→     *  * @param orderInGroup  the order of this config in the group\n   147→     * @param width         the width of the config\n   148→     * @param displayName   the name suitable for display\n   149→     * @param dependents    the configurations that are dependents of this configuration\n   150→     * @param recommender   the recommender provides valid values given the parent configuration values\n   151→     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   152→     */\n   153→    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, Validator validator, Importance importance, String documentation,\n   154→                            String group, int orderInGroup, Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents, Recommender recommender) {\n   155→        return /**\n   159→     * Define a new configuration\n   160→     * @param name               the name of the config parameter\n   161→     * @param type               the type of the config\n   162→     *               String group, int orderInGroup, Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents, Recommender recommender,\n   177→                            String alternativeString) {\n   178→        return define(new ConfigKey(name, type, defaultValue, validator, importance, documentation, group, orderInGroup, width, displayName, dependents, recommender, false, alternativeString));\n   179→    }\n   180→\n   181→    /**\n   182→     * Define a new configuration with no custom recommender\n   183→     * @param name          the name of the config parameter\n   184→     * @param type configs persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/{brokerId}</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    57→  *       using AdminClient using the resource name brokerId.</li>\n    58→  *   <li>Cluster-wide defaults persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    59→  *       using AdminClient using an empty resource name.</li>\n    60→  * </ul>\n    61→  * The order of precedence for broker configs is:\n    62→  * <ol>\n    63→  *   <li>DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/{brokerId}</li>\n    64→  *   <li>DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</li>\n    65→  *   <li>STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG: properties that broker is started up with, typically from server.properties file</li>\n    66→  *   <li>DEFAULT_CONFIG: Default configs defined in KafkaConfig</li>\n    67→  * </ol>\n    68→  * Log configs use topic config overrides if defined and fallback to broker defaults using the order of precedence above.\n    69→  * Topic config overrides may use a different config name from the default broker config.\n    70→  * See [[org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig#TopicConfigSynonyms]] for the mapping.\n    71→  * <p>\n    72→  * AdminClient returns all config synonyms in the order of precedence when configs are described with\n    73→  * <code>includeSynonyms</code>. In addition to configs that may be defined with the same name at different levels,\n    74→  * some configs have additional synonyms.\n    75→  * </p>\n    76→  * <ul>\n    77→  *   <li>Listener configs may be defined using the prefix <tt>listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}</tt>. These may be\n    78→  *       configured as dynamic or static broker configs. Listener configs have higher precedence than the base configs\n    79→  *       that don't specify the listener name. Listeners without a listener config use the base config. Base configs\n    80→  *       may be defined only as STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG or DEFAULT_CONFIG and cannot be updated dynamically.<li>\n    81→  *   <li>Some configs may be defined using multiple properties. At the moment, only listener configs are considered for reloading.\n   362→   */\n   363→  private[server] def reloadUpdatedFilesWithoutConfigChange(newProps: Properties): Unit = 391→    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   392→\n   393→    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   394→      if (value != null) {\n   395→ level will be ignored\")\n   424→\n   425→    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   426→      if (value != null) {\n   427→        try {\n   428→          props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n   429→        } catch {\n   430→          case e: Exception =>\n   431→            error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName 438→      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   439→        decodePassword(name, value)\n   440→    }\n   441→    props\n   442→  }\n   443→\n   444→  // If the secret has changed, password.encoder.old.secret contains the old secret that was used\n   445→  // to encode the configs in ZK. Decode passwords using the old secret and update ZK with values\n   446→  // encoded using the current secret. Ignore any errors during decoding since old secret may not\n   447→  // have been removed during broker restart.\n   448→  private def maybeReEncodePasswords(persistentProps: Properties, (isPasswordConfig(configName) && value != null) {\n   454→            val decoded = try {\n   455→              Some(passwordDecoder.decode(value).value)\n   456→            } catch {\n   457→           }\n   461→            decoded.foreach(value => props.put(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value))))\n   462→          }\n   463→        }\n   464→        adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Some(kafkaConfig.brokerId), props)\n   465→      }\n   466→    }\n   467→    props\n   468→  }\n   469→\n   470→  /**\n   471→   * Validate the provided configs
