This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.\n    24→broker.id=0\n    25→\n    26→############################# Socket Server Settings #############################\n    27→\n    28→# The address the socket server listens on. If not configured, the host name will be equal to the value of\n    29→# java.net.InetAddress.getCanonicalHostName(), with PLAINTEXT listener name, and port 9092.\n    30→#   FORMAT:\n    31→#     listeners = listener_name://host_name:port\n    32→#   EXAMPLE:\n    33→#     listeners = PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    34→#listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092\n    35→\n    36→# Listener name, hostname and port the broker will advertise to clients.\n    37→# If not set, it uses the value for \"listeners\".\n    38→#advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    39→\n    40→# Maps listener names to security protocols, the default is for them to be the same. See the config documentation for more details\n    41→#listener.security.protocol.map=PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL,SASL_PLAINTEXT:SASL_PLAINTEXT,SASL_SSL:SASL_SSL\n    42→\n    43→# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network\n    44→num.network.threads=3\n    45→\n    46→# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O\n    47→num.io.threads=8\n    48→\n    49→# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server\n    50→socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\n    51→\n    52→# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server\n    53→socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\n    54→\n    55→# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)\n    56→socket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n    57→\n    58→\n    59→############################# Log Basics #############################\n    60→\n    61→# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files\n    62→log.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs\n    63→\n    64→# The default number of log partitions per topic. More partitions allow greater\n    65→# parallelism for consumption, but this will also result in more files across\n    66→# the brokers.\n    67→num.partitions=1\n    68→\n    69→# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown.\n    70→# This value is recommended to be increased for installations with data dirs located in RAID array.\n    71→num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n    72→\n    73→############################# Internal Topic Settings  #############################\n    74→# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topics \"__consumer_offsets\" and \"__transaction_state\"\n    75→# For anything other than development testing, a value greater than 1 is recommended to ensure availability such as 3.\n    76→offsets.topic.replication.factor=1\n    77→transaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n    78→transaction.state.log.min.isr=1\n    79→\n    80→############################# Log Flush Policy #############################\n    81→\n    82→# Messages are immediately written to the filesystem but by default we only fsync() to sync\n    83→# the OS cache lazily. The following configurations control the flush of data to disk.\n    84→# There are a few important trade-offs here:\n    85→#    1. Durability: Unflushed data may be lost if you are not using replication.\n    86→#    2. Latency: Very large flush intervals may lead to latency spikes when the flush does occur as there will be a lot of data to flush.\n    87→#    3. Throughput: The flush is generally the most expensive operation, and a small flush interval may lead to excessive seeks.\n    88→# The settings below allow one to configure the flush policy to flush data after a period of time or\n    89→# every N messages (or both). This can be done globally and overridden on a per-topic basis.\n    90→\n    91→# The number of messages to accept before forcing a flush of data to disk\n    92→#log.flush.interval.messages=10000\n    93→\n    94→# The maximum amount of time a message can sit in a log before we force a flush\n    95→#log.flush.interval.ms=1000\n    96→\n    97→############################# Log Retention Policy #############################\n    98→\n    99→# The following configurations control the disposal of log segments. The policy can\n   100→# be set to delete segments after a period of time, or after a given size has accumulated.\n   101→# A segment will be deleted whenever *either* of these criteria are met. Deletion always happens\n   102→# from the end of the log.\n   103→\n   104→# The minimum age of a log file to be eligible for deletion due to age\n   105→log.retention.hours=168\n   106→\n   107→# A size-based retention policy for logs. Segments are pruned from the log unless the remaining\n   108→# segments drop below log.retention.bytes. Functions independently of log.retention.hours.\n   109→#log.retention.bytes=1073741824\n   110→\n   111→# The maximum size of a log segment file. When this size is reached a new log segment will be created.\n   112→#log.segment.bytes=1073741824\n   113→\n   114→# The interval at which log segments are checked to see if they can be deleted according\n   115→# to the retention policies\n   116→log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000\n   117→\n   118→############################# Zookeeper #############################\n   119→\n   120→# Zookeeper connection string (see zookeeper docs for details).\n   121→# This is a comma separated host:port pairs, each corresponding to a zk\n   122→# server. e.g. \"127.0.0.1:3000,127.0.0.1:3001,127.0.0.1:3002\".\n   123→# You can also append an optional chroot string to the urls to specify the\n   124→# root directory for all kafka znodes.\n   125→zookeeper.connect=localhost:2181\n   126→\n   127→# Timeout in ms for connecting to zookeeper\n   128→zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000\n   129→\n   130→\n   131→############################# Group Coordinator Settings #############################\n   132→\n   133→# The following configuration specifies the time, in milliseconds, that the GroupCoordinator will delay the initial consumer rebalance.\n   134→# The rebalance will be further delayed by the value of group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms as new members join the group, up to a maximum of max.poll.interval.ms.\n   135→# The default value for this is 3 seconds.\n   136→# We override this to 0 here as it makes for a better out-of-the-box experience for development and testing.\n   137→# However, in production environments the default value of 3 seconds is more suitable as this will help to avoid unnecessary, and potentially expensive, rebalances during application startup.\n   138→group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=0\n   139→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n   519→    if (reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.exists(key => oldConfig.originals.get(key) != newConfig.get(key)))\n   520→      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfig)\n   521→  }\n   522→\n   523→  /**\n   524→   * Returns the change in configurations between the new props and current props by returning a\n   525→   * map of the changed configs, as well as the set of deleted keys\n   526→   */\n   527→  private def updatedConfigs(newProps: java.util.Map[String, _],\n   528→                             currentProps: java.util.Map[String, _]): (mutable.Map[String, _], Set[String]) = {\n   529→    val changeMap = newProps.asScala.filter {\n   530→      case (k, v) => v != currentProps.get(k)\n   531→    }\n   532→    val deletedKeySet = currentProps.asScala.filter {\n   533→      case (k, _) => !newProps.containsKey(k)\n   534→    }.keySet\n   535→    (changeMap, deletedKeySet)\n   536→  }\n   537→\n   538→  /**\n   539→    * Updates values in `props` with the new values from `propsOverride`. Synonyms of updated configs\n   540→    * are removed from `props` to ensure that the config with the higher precedence is applied. For example,\n   541→    * if `log.roll.ms` was defined in server.properties and `log.roll.hours` is configured dynamically,\n   542→    * `log.roll.hours` from the dynamic configuration will be used and `log.roll.ms` will be removed from\n   543→    * `props` (even though `log.roll.hours` is secondary to `log.roll.ms`).\n   544→    */\n   545→  private def overrideProps(props: mutable.Map[String, String], propsOverride: mutable.Map[String, String]): Unit = {\n   546→    propsOverride.forKeyValue { (k, v) =>\n   547→      // Remove synonyms of `k` to ensure the right precedence is applied. But disable `matchListenerOverride`\n   548→      // so that base configs corresponding to listener configs are not removed. Base configs should not be removed\n   549→      // since they may be used by other listeners. It is ok to retain them in `props` since base configs cannot be\n   550→      // dynamically updated and listener-specific configs have the higher precedence.\n   551→      brokerConfigSynonyms(k, matchListenerOverride = false).foreach(props.remove)\n   552→      props.put(k, v)\n   553→    }\n   554→  }\n   555→\n   556→  private def updateCurrentConfig(doLog: Boolean): Unit = {\n   557→    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   558→    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   559→    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   560→    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   561→\n   562→    val oldConfig = currentConfig\n   563→    val (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate) = processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = false, doLog)\n   564→    if (newConfig ne currentConfig) {\n   565→      currentConfig = newConfig\n   566→      kafkaConfig.updateCurrentConfig(newConfig)\n   567→\n   568→      // Process BrokerReconfigurable updates after current config is updated\n   569→      brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.foreach(_.reconfigure(oldConfig, newConfig))\n   570→    }\n   571→  }\n   572→\n   573→  private def processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n   574→    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n   575→    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n   576→    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n   577→      try {\n   578→        val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig) // non-Kafka configs\n   579→        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n   580→        reconfigurables.forEach {\n   581→          case listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   582→            processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, newConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly, reloadOnly = false)\n   583→          case reconfigurable =>\n   584→            if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n   585→              processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   586→        }\n   587→\n   588→        // BrokerReconfigurable updates are processed after config is updated. Only do the validation here.\n   589→        val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   590→        brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n   591→          if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n   592→            reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n   593→            if (!validateOnly)\n   594→              brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n   595→          }\n   596→        }\n   597→        (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n   598→      } catch {\n   599→        case e: Exception =>\n   600→          if (!validateOnly)\n   601→            error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n   602→                  s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n   603→          throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n   604→      }\n   605→    }\n   606→    else\n   607→      (currentConfig, List.empty)\n   608→  }\n   609→\n   610→  private def needsReconfiguration(reconfigurableConfigs: util.Set[String], updatedKeys: Set[String], deletedKeys: Set[String]): Boolean = {\n   611→    reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(updatedKeys).nonEmpty ||\n   612→      reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(deletedKeys).nonEmpty\n   613→  }\n   614→\n   615→  private def processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable,\n   616→                                            newConfig: KafkaConfig,\n   617→                                            customConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   618→                                            validateOnly: Boolean,\n   619→                                            reloadOnly:  Boolean): Unit = {\n   620→    val listenerName = listenerReconfigurable.listenerName\n   621→    val oldValues = currentConfig.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   622→    val newValues = newConfig.valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   623→    val (changeMap, deletedKeys) = updatedConfigs(newValues, oldValues)\n   624→    val updatedKeys = changeMap.keySet\n   625→    val configsChanged = needsReconfiguration(listenerReconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, updatedKeys, deletedKeys)\n   626→    // if `reloadOnly`, reconfigure if configs haven't changed. Otherwise reconfigure if configs have changed\n   627→    if (reloadOnly != configsChanged)\n   628→      processReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, updatedKeys, newValues, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   629→  }\n   630→\n   631→  private def processReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable,\n   632→                                    updatedConfigNames: Set[String],\n   633→                                    allNewConfigs: util.Map[String, _],\n   634→                                    newCustomConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   635→                                    validateOnly: Boolean): Unit = {\n   636→    val newConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object]\n   637→    allNewConfigs.forEach((k, v) => newConfigs.put(k, v.asInstanceOf[AnyRef]))\n   638→    newConfigs.putAll(newCustomConfigs)\n   639→    try {\n   640→      reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfigs)\n   641→    } catch {\n   642→      case e: ConfigException => throw e\n   643→      case _: Exception =>\n   644→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Validation of dynamic config update of $updatedConfigNames failed with class ${reconfigurable.getClass}\")\n   645→    }\n   646→\n   647→    if (!validateOnly) {\n   648→      info(s\"Reconfiguring $reconfigurable, updated configs: $updatedConfigNames \" +\n   649→           s\"custom configs: ${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newCustomConfigs, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\")\n   650→      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfigs)\n   651→    }\n   652→  }\n   653→}\n   654→\n   655→trait BrokerReconfigurable {\n   656→\n   657→  def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]\n   658→\n   659→  def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   660→\n   661→  def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   662→}\n   663→\n   664→object DynamicLogConfig {\n   665→  /**\n   666→   * The log configurations that are non-reconfigurable. This set contains the names you\n   667→   * would use when setting a dynamic configuration on a topic, which are different than the\n   668→   * corresponding broker configuration names.\n   669→   *\n   670→   * For now, message.format.version is not reconfigurable, since we need to check that\n   671→   * the version is supported on all brokers in the cluster.\n   672→   */\n   673→  val NonReconfigrableLogConfigs: Set[String] = Set(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   674→\n   675→  /**\n   676→   * The broker configurations pertaining to logs that are reconfigurable. This set contains\n   677→   * the names you would use when setting a static or dynamic broker configuration (not topic\n   678→   * configuration).\n   679→   */\n   680→  val ReconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] =\n   681→    ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS.asScala.\n   682→      filterNot(s => NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.contains(s._1)).values.toSet\n   683→}\n   684→\n   685→class DynamicLogConfig(logManager: LogManager, server: KafkaBroker) extends BrokerReconfigurable with Logging {\n   686→\n   687→  override def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] = {\n   688→    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   689→  }\n   690→\n   691→  override def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   692→    // For update of topic config overrides, only config names and types are validated\n   693→    // Names and types have already been validated. For consistency with topic config\n   694→    // validation, no additional validation is performed.\n   695→\n   696→    def validateLogLocalRetentionMs(): Unit = {\n   697→      val logRetentionMs = newConfig.logRetentionTimeMillis\n   698→      val logLocalRetentionMs: java.lang.Long = newConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionMs\n   699→      if (logRetentionMs != -1L && logLocalRetentionMs != -2L) {\n   700→        if (logLocalRetentionMs == -1L) {\n   701→          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_MS_PROP, logLocalRetentionMs,\n   702→            s\"Value must not be -1 as ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG} value is set as $logRetentionMs.\")\n   703→        }\n   704→        if (logLocalRetentionMs > logRetentionMs) {\n   705→          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_MS_PROP, logLocalRetentionMs,\n   706→            s\"Value must not be more than VALIDATION FRAMEWORK\n\n### Validator Interface\n```java\npublic interface Validator {\n    void ensureValid(String name, Object value) throws ConfigException;\n}\n```\n\n### Built-in Validators\n\n#### Range Validator\n```java\npublic static class Range implements Validator {\n    public static Range atLeast(Number min)      // min <= value\n    public static Range between(Number min, Number max)  // min <= value <= max\n    \n    public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n        Number n = (Number) o;\n        if (min != null && n.doubleValue() < min.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be at least \" + min);\n        if (max != null && n.doubleValue() > max.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be no more than \" + max);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### String Validators\n```java\npublic static class ValidString implements Validator\npublic static class CaseInsensitiveValidString Resolves config variables (ConfigProvider)\n2. Parses all configs using `ConfigDef.parse()` (type conversion + validation)\n3. Post-processes parsed config\n4. Re-validates after post-processing\n5. Logs configuration (if `doLog=true`)\n\n### Synonym Handling\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 162-172)\n\n```scala\ndef populateSynonyms(input: util.Map[_, _]): util.Map[Any, Any] = {\n    val output = new util.HashMap[Any, Any](input)\n    val brokerId = output.get(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n    val nodeId = output.get(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n    \n    if (brokerId == null && nodeId != null) {\n        output.put(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG, nodeId)\n    } else if (brokerId != null && nodeId == null) {\n        output.put(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG, brokerId)\n    }\n    output\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. CONFIGURATION VALIDATION\n\n### Static Validation\nHappens during broker startup in `KafkaConfig.validateValues()`:\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 855-1093)\n\nExamples of validations:\n- broker.id must be >= 0\n- log.dirs must not be empty\n- listener configurations must be valid\n- security configurations must be consistent\n- KRaft-specific validations (process.roles, quorum settings)\n- Inter-broker protocol version compatibility\n- Listener name consistency\n\n### Dynamic Validation\nHappens when dynamic configs are updated via AdminClient:\n\n```scala\nprivate def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n    try {\n        validateConfigTypes(props)\n        props.asScala\n    } catch {\n        case e: Exception =>\n            val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n                val props1 = new Properties\n                props1.put(k, v)\n                try {\n                    validateConfigTypes(props1)\n                    false\n                } catch {\n                    case _: Exception => true\n                }\n            }\n            invalidProps.keys.foreach(props.remove)\n            error(s\"Dynamic config contains invalid values in: ${invalidProps.keys}, these configs will be ignored\", e)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Per-Broker vs Cluster-Level Configs\n```scala\nprivate val ClusterLevelListenerConfigs = Set(\n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG\n)\n\nprivate val PerBrokerConfigs = (DynamicSecurityConfigs ++ \n    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs).diff(ClusterLevelListenerConfigs)\n```\n\nPer-broker configs can only be set at broker level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/{brokerId}`)\nCluster-level configs can be set at default level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/<default>`)\n\n---\n\n## 7. PASSWORD ENCRYPTION FOR DYNAMIC CONFIGS\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 218-222, 376-405, 407-442, 444-468)\n\nDynamic configuration passwords are encrypted:\n\n```scala\nprivate def toPersistentProps(configProps: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            if (!perBrokerConfig)\n                throw new ConfigException(\"Password config can be defined only at broker level\")\n            props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value)))\n        }\n    }\n    \n    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            encodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n\nprivate[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n                                       perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n    \n    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            try {\n                props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n            } catch {\n                case e: Exception =>\n                    error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n                    props.remove(configName)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    \n    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            decodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. CONFIGURATION METADATA\n\nEach configuration has metadata:\n\n```java\npublic static class ConfigKey {\n    public final String name;              // e.g., \"broker.id\"\n    public final Type type;                // e.g., Type.INT\n    public final String documentation;    // Help text\n    public final Object defaultValue;     // Default value\n    public final Validator validator;     // Validation logic\n    public final Importance importance;   // HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW\n    public final String group;            // Configuration group\n    public final int orderInGroup;        // CONFIGURATION DEFINITION PATTERN\n\n### ConfigDef Class\n**File**: `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java`\n\nThe `ConfigDef` class is the core framework for defining configurations with rich metadata:\n\n```java\npublic class ConfigDef {\n    private final Map<String, ConfigKey> configKeys;\n    \n    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, \n                           Validator validator, Importance importance, \n                           String documentation, String group, int orderInGroup,\n                           Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents,\n                           Recommender recommender)\n}\n```\n\n### Configuration Types (Enum Type)\n- **BOOLEAN**: Boolean values (true/false)\n- **STRING**: String values\n- **INT**: 32-bit integer values\n- **SHORT**: 16-bit integer values\n- **LONG**: 64-bit integer values\n- **DOUBLE**: Double precision floating-point values\n- **LIST**: Comma-separated list of strings\n- **CLASS**: Java class names (loaded dynamically)\n- **PASSWORD**: Sensitive string data (hidden in logs)\n\n### Example Definition (ServerConfigs)\n**File**: `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfigs.java`\n\n```java\npublic static final String BROKER_ID_CONFIG = \"broker.id\";\npublic static final int BROKER_ID_DEFAULT = -1;\npublic static final String BROKER_ID_DOC = \"The broker id for this server...\";\n\npublic static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF = new ConfigDef()\n    .define(BROKER_ID_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_ID_DEFAULT, HIGH, BROKER_ID_DOC)\n    .define(MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, LogConfig.DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES, \n            atLeast(0), HIGH, MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n    .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE,\n            ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), \n            HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC);\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VALIDATION FRAMEWORK\n\n### Validator Interface\n```java\npublic interface Validator {\n    void ensureValid(String name, Object value) throws ConfigException;\n}\n```\n\n### Built-in Validators\n\n#### Range Validator\n```java\npublic static class Range implements Validator {\n    public static Range atLeast(Number min)      // min <= value\n    public static Range between(Number min, Number max)  // min <= value <= max\n    \n    public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n        Number n = (Number) o;\n        if (min != null && n.doubleValue() < min.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be at least \" + min);\n        if (max != null && n.doubleValue() > max.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be no more than \" + max);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### String Validators\n```java\npublic static class ValidString implements Validator\npublic static class CaseInsensitiveValidString implements Validator  \npublic static class ValidList implements Validator\npublic static class NonEmptyString implements Validator\npublic static class NonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars implements Validator\npublic static class NonNullValidator implements Validator\n```\n\n#### List Size Validator\n```java\npublic static class ListSize implements Validator {\n    public static ListSize atMostOfSize(final int maxSize)\n}\n```\n\n#### Composite & Lambda Validators\n```java\npublic static class CompositeValidator implements Validator {\n    public static CompositeValidator of(Validator... validators)\n}\n\npublic static class LambdaValidator implements Validator {\n    public static LambdaValidator with(BiConsumer<String, Object> ensureValid,\n                                      Supplier<String> toStringFunction)\n}\n```\n\n### Validation Process\n**File**: `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java`\n\n```java\npublic AbstractConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals, ...) {\n    Map<String, Object> originalMap = Utils.castToStringObjectMap(originals);\n    this.originals = resolveConfigVariables(configProviderProps, originalMap);\n    this.values = definition.parse(this.originals);  // Type parsing + validation\n    Map<String, Object> configUpdates = postProcessParsedConfig(...);\n    this.values.putAll(configUpdates);\n    definition.parse(this.values);  // Re-validate after post-processing\n}\n```\n\nType parsing and validator conversion\n    } else if (NO_DEFAULT_VALUE.equals(key.defaultValue)) {\n        throw new ConfigException(\"Missing required configuration...\");\n    } else {\n        parsedValue = processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, \n                                  doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n    \n    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n        try {\n            val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig)\n            newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n            \n            // Validate and reconfigure Reconfigurable components\n            reconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n                if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n                    processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, \n                                        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n            }\n            \n            // Validate BrokerReconfigurable components (reconfigure after config update)\n            val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n            brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n                if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, \n                                        changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n                    reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n                    if (!validateOnly)\n                        brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n                }\n            }\n            \n            (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n        } catch {\n            case e: Exception =>\n                if (!validateOnly)\n                    error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n                          s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n                throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n        }\n    } else {\n        (currentConfig, List.empty)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Config Synonyms and Precedence\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 122-140)\n\nSome configurations have multiple names (synonyms):\n```scala\ndef brokerConfigSynonyms(name: String, matchListenerOverride: Boolean): List[String] = {\n    name match {\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n            List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG | List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n        case mechanismConfig.getOrElse(baseName))\n        case _ => List(name)\n    }\n}\n```\n\nListener-specific configs override base configs:\n- Pattern: `listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}`\n- Example: `listener.name.PLAINTEXT.ssl.keystore.location`\n\n---\n\n## 5. CONFIGURATION FILES AND LOADING\n\n### Server Properties File\n**File**: `/workspace/config/server.properties`\n\nExample broker configuration structure:\n```properties\n# General\nbroker.id=0\n\n# Socket Server Settings\nlisteners=PLAINTEXT://:9092\nadvertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\nnum.network.threads=3\nnum.io.threads=8\nsocket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\nsocket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\nsocket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n\n# Log Configuration\nlog.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs\nnum.partitions=1\nnum.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n\n# Internal Topics\noffsets.topic.replication.factor=1\ntransaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n\n# Replication\ndefault.replication.factor=3\n\n# Log Retention\nlog.retention.hours=168\nlog.retention.check.interval.ms=300000\n\n# ZooKeeper\nzookeeper.connect=localhost:2181\nzookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000\n\n# Group Coordinator\ngroup.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=0\n```\n\n### KafkaConfig Creation\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 97-111)\n\n```scala\ndef fromProps(props: Properties): KafkaConfig = fromProps(props, true)\n\ndef fromProps(props: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig =\n    new KafkaConfig(props, doLog)\n\ndef fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties): KafkaConfig =\n    fromProps(defaults, overrides, true)\n\ndef fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig = {\n    val props = new Properties()\n    props ++= defaults\n    props ++= overrides\n    fromProps(props, doLog)\n}\n```\n\n### Configuration Parsing\n```scala\nclass KafkaConfig private(doLog: Boolean, val props: util.Map[_, _])\n    extends AbstractKafkaConfig(KafkaConfig.configDef, props, \n                               Utils.castToStringObjectMap(props), doLog) \n```\n\nThe `AbstractKafkaConfig` constructor:\n1. Resolves config variables (ConfigProvider)\n2. Parses all configs using `ConfigDef.parse()` (type conversion + validation)\n3. Post-processes parsed config\n4. Re-validates after post-processing\n5. Logs configuration (if `doLog=true`)\n\n### Synonym Handling\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 162-172)\n\n```scala\ndef populateSynonyms(input: util.Map[_, _]): util.Map[Any, Any] = {\n    val output = new util.HashMap[Any, Any](input)\n    val brokerId = output.get(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n    val nodeId = output.get(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n    \n    if (brokerId == null && nodeId != null) {\n        output.put(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG, nodeId)\n    } else if (brokerId != null && nodeId == null) {\n        output.put(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG, brokerId)\n    }\n    output\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. CONFIGURATION VALIDATION\n\n### Static Validation\nHappens during broker startup in `KafkaConfig.validateValues()`:\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 855-1093)\n\nExamples of validations:\n- broker.id must be >= 0\n- log.dirs must not be empty\n- listener configurations must be valid\n- security configurations must be consistent\n- KRaft-specific validations (process.roles, quorum settings)\n- Inter-broker protocol version compatibility\n- Listener name consistency\n\n### Dynamic Validation\nHappens when dynamic configs are updated via AdminClient:\n\n```scala\nprivate def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n    try {\n        validateConfigTypes(props)\n        props.asScala\n    } catch {\n        case e: Exception =>\n            val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n                val props1 = new Properties\n                props1.put(k, v)\n                try {\n                    validateConfigTypes(props1)\n                    false\n                } catch {\n                    case _: Exception => true\n                }\n            }\n            invalidProps.keys.foreach(props.remove)\n            error(s\"Dynamic config contains invalid values in: ${invalidProps.keys}, these configs will be ignored\", e)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Per-Broker vs Cluster-Level Configs\n```scala\nprivate val ClusterLevelListenerConfigs = Set(\n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG\n)\n\nprivate val PerBrokerConfigs = (DynamicSecurityConfigs ++ \n    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs).diff(ClusterLevelListenerConfigs)\n```\n\nPer-broker configs can only be set at broker level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/{brokerId}`)\nCluster-level configs can be set at default level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/<default>`)\n\n---\n\n## 7. PASSWORD ENCRYPTION FOR DYNAMIC CONFIGS\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 218-222, 376-405, 407-442, 444-468)\n\nDynamic configuration passwords are encrypted:\n\n```scala\nprivate def toPersistentProps(configProps: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            if (!perBrokerConfig)\n                throw new ConfigException(\"Password config can be defined only at broker level\")\n            props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value)))\n        }\n    }\n    \n    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            encodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n\nprivate[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n                                       perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n    \n    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            try {\n                props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n            } catch {\n                case e: Exception =>\n                    error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n                    props.remove(configName)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    \n    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            decodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. CONFIGURATION METADATA\n\nEach configuration has metadata:\n\n```java\npublic static class ConfigKey {\n    public final String name;              // e.g., \"broker.id\"\n    public final Type type;                // e.g., Type.INT\n    public final String documentation;    // Help text\n    public final Object defaultValue;     // Default value\n    public final Validator validator;     // Validation logic\n    public final Importance importance;   // HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW\n    public final String group;            // Configuration group\n    public final int orderInGroup;        // Order in UI\n    public final Width width;             // UI width (NONE, SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG)\n    public final String displayName;      // Display name for UI\n    public final List<String> dependents; // Configs that depend on this one\n    public final Recommender recommender; // Suggests valid values based on other configs\n    public final boolean internal;        // Internal/hidden from documentation\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nThe Kafka configuration system is sophisticated and well-structured:\n\n1. **Central Definition**: All configs defined in modular ConfigDef classes\n2. **Type-Safe**: Strong typing with conversion and validation\n3. **Flexible Validation**: Extensible validator framework with built-in validators\n4. **Dynamic Updates**: Supports updating configs without broker restart\n5. **Precedence Management**: Clear order of precedence for multiple config sources\n6. **Security**: Password encryption for sensitive dynamic configs\n7. **Rich Metadata**: Detailed configuration metadata for documentation and UI\n8. **Reconfigurable Components**: Clean interface for components to react to config changes\n9. **Synonyms**: Support for configuration aliases with proper precedence handling\n10. **Comprehensive Validation**: Both static (startup) and dynamic validation with detailed error messages"}],"totalDurationMs":73023,"totalTokens":102497,"totalToolUseCount":29,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4734,"cache_read_input_tokens":91197,"output_tokens":6559,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4734,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    52→\n    53→/**\n    54→  * Dynamic broker configurations are stored in ZooKeeper and may be defined at two levels:\n    55→  * <ul>\n    56→  *   <li>Per-broker configs persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/{brokerId}</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    57→  *       using AdminClient using the resource name brokerId.</li>\n    58→  *   <li>Cluster-wide defaults persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    59→  *       using AdminClient using an empty resource name.</li>\n    60→  * </ul>\n    61→  * The order of precedence for broker configs is:\n    62→  * <ol>\n    63→  *   <li>DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/{brokerId}</li>\n    64→  *   <li>DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</li>\n    65→  *   <li>STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG: properties that broker is started up with, typically from server.properties file</li>\n    66→  *   <li>DEFAULT_CONFIG: Default configs defined in KafkaConfig</li>\n    67→  * </ol>\n    68→  * Log configs use topic config overrides if defined and fallback to broker defaults using the order of precedence above.\n    69→  * Topic config overrides may use a different config name from the default broker config.\n    70→  * See [[org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig#TopicConfigSynonyms]] for the mapping.\n    71→  * <p>\n    72→  * AdminClient returns all config synonyms in the order of precedence when configs are described with\n    73→  * <code>includeSynonyms</code>. In addition to configs that may be defined with the same name at different levels,\n    74→  * some configs have additional synonyms.\n    75→  * </p>\n    76→  * <ul>\n    77→  *   <li>Listener configs may be defined using the prefix <tt>listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}</tt>. These may be\n    78→  *       configured as dynamic or static broker configs. Listener configs have higher precedence than the base configs\n    79→  *       that don't specify the listener name. Listeners without a listener config use the base config. Base configs\n    80→  *       may be defined only as STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG or DEFAULT_CONFIG and cannot be updated dynamically.<li>\n    81→  *   <li>Some configs may be defined using multiple properties. For example, <tt>log.roll.ms</tt> and\n    82→  *       <tt>log.roll.hours</tt> refer to the same config that may be defined in milliseconds or hours. The order of\n    83→  *       precedence of these synonyms is described in the docs of these configs in [[kafka.server.KafkaConfig]].</li>\n    84→  * </ul>\n    85→  *\n    86→  */\n    87→object These are reconfigured before `KafkaConfig` is updated so that\n   254→   * the update can be aborted if `reconfigure()` fails with an exception.\n   255→   *\n   256→   * `BrokerReconfigurable` is used for internal reconfigurable classes. 354→\n   355→  /**\n   356→   * All config updates through ZooKeeper are triggered through actual changes in values stored in ZooKeeper.\n   357→   * For some configs like SSL keystores and truststores, we also want to reload the store if it was modified\n   358→   * in-place, even though the actual value of the file path and password haven't changed. This scenario alone\n   359→   * is handled here when a config update request using admin client is processed by ZkAdminManager. If any of\n   360→   * the SSL configs have changed, then the update will not be done here, but will be handled later when ZK\n   361→   * changes are processed. At the moment, only listener configs are considered for reloading.\n   362→   */\n   363→  private[server] def reloadUpdatedFilesWithoutConfigChange(newProps: Properties): Unit = .foreach {\n   367→        case reconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   368→          val kafkaProps = validatedKafkaProps(newProps, perBrokerConfig = true)\n   369→          val newConfig = new reconfigurable =>\n   372→          trace(s\"Files will not be reloaded without config change for $reconfigurable\")\n   373→      }\n   374→  }\n   375→\n   376→  private def 391→    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   392→\n   393→    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   394→      if (value != null) {\n   395→ Password(value)))\n   398→      }\n   399→    }\n   400→    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   401→      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   402→        encodePassword(name, value)\n   403→    }\n   404→    props\n   405→  }\n   406→\n   407→  private[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n   408→                                          perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n   409→    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   410→\n   411→    // Remove all invalid configs from `props`\n   412→    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n   413→    def removeInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n   414→      if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty) {\n   415→        invalidPropNames.foreach(props.remove)\n   416→        error(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n   417→      }\n   418→    }\n   419→    removeInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Non-dynamic configs configured in ZooKeeper will be ignored\")\n   420→    removeInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n   421→      \"Security configs can be dynamically updated only using listener prefix, base configs will be ignored\")\n   422→    if (!perBrokerConfig)\n   423→      removeInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props), \"Per-broker configs defined at default cluster level will be ignored\")\n   424→\n   425→    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   426→      if (value != null) {\n   427→        try {\n   428→          props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n   429→        } catch {\n   430→          case e: Exception =>\n   431→            error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n   432→            props.remove(configName)\n   433→        }\n   434→      }\n   435→    }\n   436→\n   437→    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   438→      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   439→        decodePassword(name, value)\n   440→    }\n   441→    props\n   442→  }\n   443→\n   444→  // If the secret has changed, password.encoder.old.secret contains the old secret that was used\n   445→  // to encode the configs in ZK. Decode passwords using the old secret and update ZK with values\n   446→  // encoded using the current secret. Ignore any errors during decoding since old secret may not\n   447→  // have been removed during broker restart.\n   448→  private def maybeReEncodePasswords(persistentProps: Properties, adminZkClient: AdminZkClient): Properties = {\n   449→    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   450→    if (props.asScala.keySet.exists(isPasswordConfig)) {\n   451→      maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderOldSecret).foreach { passwordDecoder =>\n   452→        persistentProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (configName, value) =>\n   453→          if (isPasswordConfig(configName) && value != null) {\n   454→            val decoded = try {\n   455→              Some(passwordDecoder.decode(value).value)\n   456→            } catch {\n   457→              case _: Exception =>\n   458→                debug(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded using old secret, new secret will be used.\")\n   459→                None\n   460→            }\n   461→            decoded.foreach(value => props.put(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value))))\n   462→          }\n   463→        }\n   464→        adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Some(kafkaConfig.brokerId), props)\n   465→      }\n   466→    }\n   467→    props\n   468→  }\n   469→\n   470→  /**\n   471→   * Validate the provided configs invoking this method.\n   475→   */\n   476→  private def validatedKafkaProps(propsOverride: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Map[String, String] = {\n   477→    val propsResolved = DynamicBrokerConfig.resolveVariableConfigs(propsOverride)\n   478→    validateConfigs(propsResolved, perBrokerConfig)\n   479→    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   480→    newProps ++= processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = true)\n   494→  }\n   495→\n   496→  private def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n   497→    try {\n   498→      validateConfigTypes(props)\n   499→      props.asScala\n   500→    } catch {\n   501→      case e: Exception =>\n   502→        val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n   503→ }\n   517→\n   518→  private[server] def maybeReconfigure(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable, oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n   519→    if (reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.exists(key => oldConfig.originals.get(key) != newConfig.get(key)))\n   520→      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfig)\n   521→  }\n   522→\n   523→  /**\n   524→   * Returns the change in configurations between the new props and current props by returning a\n   525→   * map of the changed configs, as well as the set of deleted keys\n   526→   */\n   527→  private def updatedConfigs(newProps: java.util.Map[String, _],\n   528→                             currentProps: java.util.Map[String, _]): (mutable.Map[String, _], Set[String]) = {\n   529→    val changeMap = newProps.asScala.filter {\n   530→      case (k, v) => v != currentProps.get(k)\n   531→    }\n   532→    val deletedKeySet = currentProps.asScala.filter {\n   533→      case (k, _) => !newProps.containsKey(k)\n   534→    }.keySet\n   535→    (changeMap, deletedKeySet)\n   536→  }\n   537→\n   538→  /**\n   539→    * Updates values in `props` with the new values from `propsOverride`. Synonyms of updated configs\n   540→    * are removed from `props` to ensure that the config with the higher precedence is applied. For example,\n   541→    * if `log.roll.ms` was defined in server.properties and `log.roll.hours` is configured dynamically,\n   542→    * `log.roll.hours` from the dynamic configuration will be used and `log.roll.ms` will be removed from\n   543→    * `props` (even though `log.roll.hours` is secondary to `log.roll.ms`).\n   544→    */\n   545→  private def overrideProps(props: mutable.Map[String, String], propsOverride: mutable.Map[String, String]): Unit = {\n   546→    propsOverride.forKeyValue { (k, v) =>\n   547→      // Remove synonyms of `k` to ensure the right precedence is applied. But disable `matchListenerOverride`\n   548→      // so that base configs corresponding to listener configs are not removed. Base configs should not be removed\n   549→      // since they may be used by other listeners. It is ok to retain them in `props` since base configs cannot be\n   550→      // dynamically updated and listener-specific configs have the higher precedence.\n   551→      brokerConfigSynonyms(k, matchListenerOverride = false).foreach(props.remove)\n   552→      props.put(k, v)\n   553→    }\n   554→  }\n   555→\n   556→  private def updateCurrentConfig(doLog: Boolean): Unit = {\n   557→    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   558→    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   559→    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   560→    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   561→\n   562→    val oldConfig = currentConfig\n   563→    val (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate) = processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = false, doLog)\n   564→    if (newConfig ne currentConfig) {\n   565→      currentConfig = newConfig\n   566→      kafkaConfig.updateCurrentConfig(newConfig)\n   567→\n   568→      // Process BrokerReconfigurable updates after current config is updated\n   569→      brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.foreach(_.reconfigure(oldConfig, newConfig))\n   570→    }\n   571→  }\n   572→\n   573→  private def processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n   574→    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n   575→    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n   576→    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n   577→      try {\n   578→        val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig) // non-Kafka configs\n   579→        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n   580→        reconfigurables.forEach {\n   581→          case listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   582→            processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, newConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly, reloadOnly = false)\n   583→          case reconfigurable =>\n   584→            if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n   585→              processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   586→        }\n   587→\n   588→        // BrokerReconfigurable updates are processed after config is updated. Only do the validation here.\n   589→        val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   590→        brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n   591→          if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n   592→            reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n   593→            if (!validateOnly)\n   594→              brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n   595→          }\n   596→        }\n   597→        (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n   598→      } catch {\n   599→        case e: Exception =>\n   600→          if (!validateOnly)\n   601→            error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n   602→                  s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n   603→          throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n   604→      }\n   605→    }\n   606→    else\n   607→      (currentConfig, List.empty)\n   608→  }\n   609→\n   610→  private def needsReconfiguration(reconfigurableConfigs: util.Set[String], updatedKeys: Set[String], deletedKeys: Set[String]): Boolean = {\n   611→    reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(updatedKeys).nonEmpty ||\n   612→      reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(deletedKeys).nonEmpty\n   613→  }\n   614→\n   615→  private def processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable,\n   616→                                            newConfig: KafkaConfig,\n   617→                                            customConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   618→                                            validateOnly: Boolean,\n   619→                                            reloadOnly:  Boolean): Unit = {\n   620→    val listenerName = listenerReconfigurable.listenerName\n   621→    val oldValues = currentConfig.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   622→    val newValues = newConfig.valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   623→    val (changeMap, deletedKeys) = updatedConfigs(newValues, oldValues)\n   624→    val updatedKeys = changeMap.keySet\n   625→    val configsChanged = needsReconfiguration(listenerReconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, updatedKeys, deletedKeys)\n   626→    // if `reloadOnly`, reconfigure if configs haven't changed. Otherwise reconfigure if configs have changed\n   627→    if (reloadOnly != configsChanged)\n   628→      processReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, updatedKeys, newValues, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   629→  }\n   630→\n   631→  private def processReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable,\n   632→                                    updatedConfigNames: Set[String],\n   633→                                    allNewConfigs: util.Map[String, _],\n   634→                                    newCustomConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   635→                                    validateOnly: Boolean): Unit = {\n   636→    val newConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object]\n   637→    allNewConfigs.forEach((k, v) => newConfigs.put(k, v.asInstanceOf[AnyRef]))\n   638→    newConfigs.putAll(newCustomConfigs)\n   639→    try {\n   640→      reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfigs)\n   641→    } catch {\n   642→      case e: ConfigException => throw e\n   643→      case _: Exception =>\n   644→        throw new ConfigException(s\"Validation of dynamic config update of $updatedConfigNames failed with class ${reconfigurable.getClass}\")\n   645→    }\n   646→\n   647→    if (!validateOnly) {\n   648→      info(s\"Reconfiguring $reconfigurable, updated configs: $updatedConfigNames \" +\n   649→           s\"custom configs: ${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newCustomConfigs, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\")\n   650→      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfigs)\n   651→    }\n   652→  }\n   653→}\n   654→\n   655→trait BrokerReconfigurable {\n   656→\n   657→  def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]\n   658→\n   659→  def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   660→\n   661→  def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   662→}\n   663→\n   664→object DynamicLogConfig {\n   665→  /**\n   666→   * The log configurations that are non-reconfigurable. This set contains the names you\n   667→   * would use when setting a dynamic configuration on a topic, which are different than the\n   668→   * corresponding broker configuration names.\n   669→   *\n   670→   * For now, message.format.version is not reconfigurable, since we need to check that\n   671→   * the version is supported on all brokers in the cluster.\n   672→   */\n   673→  val NonReconfigrableLogConfigs: Set[String] = Set(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   674→\n   675→  /**\n   676→   * The broker configurations pertaining to logs that are reconfigurable. This set contains\n   677→   * the names you would use when setting a static or dynamic broker configuration (not topic\n   678→   * configuration).\n   679→   */\n   680→  val ReconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] =\n   681→    ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS.asScala.\n   682→      filterNot(s => NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.contains(s._1)).values.toSet\n   683→}\n   684→\n   685→class DynamicLogConfig(logManager: LogManager, server: KafkaBroker) extends BrokerReconfigurable with Logging {\n   686→\n   687→  override def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] = {\n   688→    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   689→  }\n   690→\n   691→  override def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   692→    // For update of topic config overrides, only config names and types are validated\n   693→    // Names and types have already been validated. For consistency with topic config\n   694→    // validation, no additional validation is performed.\n   695→\n   696→    def validateLogLocalRetentionMs(): Unit = {\n   697→      val logRetentionMs = newConfig.logRetentionTimeMillis\n   698→      val logLocalRetentionMs: java.lang.Long = newConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionMs\n   699→      if (logRetentionMs != -1L && logLocalRetentionMs != -2L) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. This must be defined for \" +\n    55→            \"the same security protocol to be usable in more than one port or IP. For example, internal and \" +\n    56→            \"external traffic can be separated even if SSL is required for both. Concretely, the user could define listeners \" +\n    57→            \"with names INTERNAL and EXTERNAL and this property as: <code>INTERNAL:SSL,EXTERNAL:SSL</code>. General server configs\n- `/workspace/server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerLogConfigs.java` - Log-related configs\n- `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ReplicationConfigs.java` - Integer values\n- `LONG` - Long values\n- `STRING` - String values\n- `BOOLEAN` - Boolean values\n- `CLASS` - Class type\n- `DOUBLE` - Double values\n\n**Validator Options:**\n\n1. **Range Validators (from `ConfigDef.Range`):**\n   ```java\n   import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Range.atLeast;\n   \n   .define(MY_CONFIG, INT, MY_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), HIGH, MY_DOC)  // value >= 0\n   .define(MY_CONFIG, INT, MY_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, MY_DOC)  // value >= 1\n   ```\n\n2. **String Validators:**\n   ```java\n   // Valid string options\n   ConfigDef.ValidString.in(option1, option2, option3)\n   \n   // Example from ServerConfigs.java (line 162):\n   .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE, \n           ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), \n           HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC)\n   ```\n\n3. **Custom Validators:**\n   ```java\n   // Example from ReplicationConfigs.java (line 174):\n   .define(INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG, STRING, \n           INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DEFAULT, \n           new MetadataVersionValidator(), \n           MEDIUM, INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DOC)\n   \n   .define(AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, STRING, \n           AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DEFAULT, \n           new ConfigDef.NonNullValidator(), \n           LOW, AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DOC)\n   ```\n\n**Importance Levels (from `ConfigDef.Importance`):**\n- `HIGH` -
